At Precis, we are a group of friendly experts in media buying, analytics & marketing science, creative, and technology with a joint mission to help great companies thrive in the digital landscape.
   

  

 As a
 **Creative Solutions Engineer (Full-stack Engineer - Cloud-native, GCP)** 
 at Precis, you will work hands-on with building and maintaining cloud-native systems that enable scalable, automated, and forward-looking creative technology solutions. You will design and implement backend services, data pipelines, and full-stack applications that support experimentation, innovation, and production-grade systems — always with a focus on scalability, security, and maintainability.
   

  

 This role sits within our Creative Tech department, working at the intersection of software engineering, cloud infrastructure, and creative problem-solving. You will build technical systems and platforms that are shaped by how creative work is produced, scaled, and evolved — requiring both strong engineering fundamentals and an understanding of creative workflows, constraints, and opportunities.
   

  

 The focus is on building flexible, future-proof solutions that enable experimentation and new ways of working, rather than optimizing for a single format or existing workflow.
   

  

 We are looking for an intermediate engineer who enjoys shipping real systems, learning through doing, and contributing to technical discussions around architecture and tooling.
   

  

 We are committed to building an inclusive and fair workplace that attracts talent and advances its people, regardless of their background, experiences, or perspectives. We believe that a diverse set of minds contributes to our innovative culture and our ability to constantly evolve.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing 💼**
**Engineering & Platform Development**
* Design, build, and maintain backend services, APIs, and data pipelines using TypeScript and Node.js.
* Build and maintain full-stack solutions, including user interfaces using React and Next.js.
* Develop and operate cloud-native solutions on Google Cloud Platform, working with services such as Cloud Run, Firebase, and other common GCP components.
* Contribute to internal platforms and tooling that enable large-scale creative production, automation, and custom workflows.
* Ensure solutions are built with strong fundamentals around architecture, security, scalability, and reliability.
* Work with Git as part of a collaborative development workflow, including code reviews and shared ownership of codebases.
* Participate in documentation and knowledge sharing to support long-term maintainability of systems.


**Solution Design, Automation & Innovation**
* Translate creative and operational needs into robust technical solutions.
* Help design and evolve systems that support creative automation, with a strong focus on exploring and applying AI in thoughtful, production-grade ways where it creates real value.
* Collaborate closely with other engineers, creative technologists, and stakeholders within Creative, such as designers and strategists.
* Continuously improve existing systems through iteration, refactoring, and technical curiosity.


**What We Are Looking For ✔️**
* Professional experience as a software engineer at an intermediate level.
* Strong fundamentals in TypeScript and backend development using Node.js.
* Experience building and maintaining full-stack applications, including frontend development with React and Next.js.
* Experience building and operating backend services, APIs, and/or data pipelines in production.
* Experience working with Google Cloud Platform.
* A solid understanding of cloud-native architecture, security basics, and system design.
* Experience working with Git in a team-based development environment.
* Ability to work independently on tasks while collaborating effectively with others.


**Nice-to-have**
* Experience with Python.
* Experience working with Figma, including building or supporting plugins and workflows.
* Experience with ExtendScript or development for creative tools such as After Effects.
* Experience with JavaScript bundlers such as Webpack, Esbuild, or similar tools.
* Familiarity with node-based automation systems such as n8n.
* Exposure to automation systems, AI-enabled solutions, or large-scale production pipelines.
* Interest in creative technology, digital advertising, or understanding what makes effective creative.


**What We Offer🌟**
*You can read our full offering in our* 
*handbook* 
*.*
* An intense learning environment where you will be able to grow and develop your skills and passions alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.
* A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.
* Transparent salary setting, you can find all our salary bands in our handbook. This role is a L2 role within the Creative Tech column.
* 30 days paid leave, flexible public holidays, parental pay, a solid pension scheme and key insurance. Our different offices also offer different perks such as massage, health care contributions, free book orders, healthy snacks & great coffee, EAP programs and your choice of PC or Mac.
* A diverse environment with a high focus on inclusion and belonging. We speak 46 native languages around Precis, and our internal inclusion score is 4.5 out of 5.


**We update all candidates on their applications by email. If you don't receive an email from us, please check your spam folder.**

title: Head of Artificial Intelligence Division (AI) - based in Luxembourg
description:
**This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** 
 The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support.
   

  

 The EIB, the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS), Digital Transformation & Innovation Department\* (DTI), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a Head of Artificial Intelligence Division (AI)\*\*. This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.
   

  

**This is a full-time position at grade 7 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.**
* new name following Directorate reorganisation effective from March 2026
* internal benchmark Division Head IT Applications


***Applicants will receive initial feedback in March/April 2026***
***Assessment Centres for selected applicants will take place in April/May 2026***
***Panel interviews are anticipated for May 2026***
 Purpose
   

  

 As Head of the Artificial Intelligence Division, you will be shaping and scaling AI capabilities across the Group. You will define and execute the Group’s AI strategy, turning business ambition into a coherent, secure and scalable AI ecosystem. You will build and operate the Group AI Hub, combining deep expertise in AI engineering and platforms with strong leadership and change management. Working closely with business and IT stakeholders, you will drive adoption of AI solutions that deliver measurable value while ensuring compliance, resilience and ethical use.
   

  

 If you are a seasoned technology leader who can bridge AI vision with real‑world delivery, enjoy building and scaling new capabilities, and care about responsible, secure and ethical use of AI, this role is for you. Join us and help shape how artificial intelligence creates tangible impact across the organisation.
   

  

 Operating Network
   

  

 The Artificial Intelligence Division designs, builds, and operates the Group’s AI capabilities, including AI platforms, engineering frameworks, model lifecycle management, and enabling services for business-driven AI use cases. The Division ensures that AI solutions are secure, compliant, scalable, and aligned with enterprise architecture and regulatory requirements.
   

  

 You will report to the Head of the Digital Transformation & Innovation Department and work closely with the other Heads of Divisions of the Directorate, as well as with business users who participate in projects and initiatives led by the Division.
   

  

 Accountabilities
   

  

* Define and lead the EIB Group’s AI strategy, aligning AI technologies, platforms and operating models with business priorities and Group‑wide digital ambitions.
* Provide leadership and direction to the Division, ensuring clear objectives, timely decision-making and effective resolution of complex issues, while fostering a culture of continuous learning and knowledge sharing.
* Build, scale and operate the Group AI Hub, establishing enterprise AI platforms and engineering foundations that enable AI adoption at scale across the organisation.
* Drive AI‑enabled transformation by partnering with senior business and IT stakeholders to embed AI into core processes and deliver measurable business value.
* Ensure trusted and responsible AI by embedding security, resilience, ethical principles and regulatory compliance (including AI Act, DORA and data protection) across all AI solutions.
* Advance the Group’s AI maturity by leveraging data as a strategic asset and enabling robust, data‑driven intelligence at enterprise level.
* Build and lead a high‑performing, multidisciplinary AI organisation, developing talent, capabilities and culture to sustain long‑term impact.
* Represent the AI Division internally and externally, contributing to governance, architecture and peer institution forums, and shaping the Group's position in the AI ecosystem.
* Manage vendors, partners and procurement in the AI domain, ensuring value for money, compliance and effective service delivery through clear SLAs and quality metrics.
* Monitor AI technology and market trends, scaling team capabilities and tooling to keep the Group at the forefront of AI innovation.


**Qualifications**
* University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in computer science, artificial intelligence, data science, engineering, or a related information technology domain.
* At least 10 years of progressive experience managing large-scale IT and digital initiatives, with exposure to AI, advanced analytics, data platforms, or large enterprise technology landscapes.
* Experience in or proven ability for managing and coaching people ideally in multicultural environments or international organisations (e.g. contribution to: deciding on team resources and skills; establishing objectives; assessing performance; delegating and supervising tasks; guiding, motivating, coaching and providing feedback).
* Experience in implementing larger scale of AI Programme at the level of the Organisation with experience in re-skilling the staff and re-engineering existing AI Platforms.
* Strong technical background in AI engineering and platforms, including machine learning, generative AI, model lifecycle management, MLOps.
* Good expertise in the modern AI landscape, including market standards, models, automation frameworks, platforms, and enterprise grade AI services
* Experience delivering secure, scalable, and resilient solutions in hybrid (on-prem/cloud) environments, with strong attention to security, data protection, and operational robustness.
* Solid business acumen, preferably in the banking or financial services sector, with the ability to translate business needs into effective AI solutions and ability to understand regulatory and risk constraints.
* Strong leadership and change management skills, with a proven track record in driving adoption of new technologies and operating models across multiple business and IT domains.
* Excellent knowledge of English. Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage\*\*\*.


 Competencies
   

  

*Find out more about EIB core and managerial competencies* 
 here
   

  

 To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here
   

  

*(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* 
*level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.*
 We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.
   

  

 We strongly invite applicants with a disability, neurodivergent profile or chronic condition to request reasonable accommodations at any stage during the recruitment process. Please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will ensure that your request is handled.
   

  

 By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.
   

  

 Deadline for applications:
 **24 March** 
**2026**

title: IT-säkerhetsspecialist
description:
Jobba för originalet.
   

  

 Välkommen till Apoteket, nummer ett sedan 1971. Med visioner för framtiden och kvalitet i varje detalj, skapar vi förutsättningar för ett liv i hälsa. Hos oss utvecklas du i en kultur där din röst alltid blir hörd. Och allt vi får över ger vi tillbaka till samhället. Det är så vi gör skillnad på riktigt.
   

  

 Om Digital infrastruktur, IT-säkerhet & drift
   

  

 På vår Tech-avdelning tror vi på att sätta människan i centrum – både våra kunder och våra medarbetare.
   

  

 I våra tvärfunktionella team ges alla möjlighet att ta initiativ och driva fram nya idéer, från koncept till produktion.
   

  

 Inom Digital infrastruktur, IT-säkerhet & drift arbetar vi för att säkerställa att Apotekets tekniska plattformar, säkerhetslösningar och driftmiljöer håller hög kvalitet, är robusta och ständigt förbättras.
   

  

 Här samarbetar arkitekter, säkerhetsfunktioner, driftleverantörer och applikationsteam för att skapa en trygg, stabil och säker digital grund för hela vår verksamhet.
   

  

 Du ingår i Apotekets säkerhets- och teknikarena tillsammans med governance-ansvarig, arkitekt, applikationsteam, driftleverantör och SOC. Tillsammans arbetar ni för att stärka Apotekets skydd mot dagens och morgondagens hot och hjälpa organisationen att fatta kloka, säkra beslut.
   

  

 Din roll
   

  

 Som IT-säkerhetsspecialist blir du en nyckelperson i Apotekets förmåga att upprätthålla en hög och modern säkerhetsnivå i vår digitala miljö. Du arbetar nära verksamheten, våra IT-team och externa partners för att omsätta våra informations- och cybersäkerhetskrav i praktiskt, operativt arbete.
   

  

 Här kommer du in i en tight organisation med många idéer, hög innovationskraft och framåtlutat tekniskt fokus, där vi tillsammans driver en kontinuerlig förbättring av Apotekets IT-säkerhet. Du kommer arbeta nära verksamheten och få stort inflytande i vardagen och möjlighet att göra verklig skillnad.
   

  

 Du kombinerar teknisk expertis med strukturerad uppföljning, riskförståelse och förmåga att driva förbättringar. Här ser du tydligt effekten av det du gör – genom minskade risker, snabbare incidentrespons, ökad säkerhetsmognad och tryggare digitala kundmöten.
   

  

 Vad du kommer göra hos oss:
   

  

* Designa, implementera och följa upp Apotekets informationssäkerhets- och cybersäkerhetskrav.
* Administrera och optimera SIEM, interna och externa sårbarhetsscanners samt andra säkerhetsverktyg för infrastruktur.
* Driva sårbarhetshantering vilket innefattar, analys, prioritering, respons och uppföljning genom att validera resultat och spåra åtgärdsframsteg i infrastruktur, applikationer och nätverk.
* Hantera dagliga säkerhetsutmaningar, incidenter, utredningar och avvikelser.
* Delta i interna och externa IT-revisioner.
* Underhålla dashboards för KPI:er, SLA:er, säkerhetsstatistik och eskalera högriskproblem till ledningen med tydlig beskrivning av teknisk och verksamhetsmässig påverkan.
* Ta ett operativt ansvar för vårt verktyg för kontinuerlig sårbarhetstestning och för penetrationstester.
* Bidra med utbildningar, dokumentation och kontinuerliga förbättringar för att ge stöd och vägledning för användarmedvetenhet om säkerhetsrutiner.
* Stödja och fatta säkerhetsbeslut i vår molnmiljö.


**Vem söker vi?**
 Vi söker dig som är analytisk, lösningsorienterad och trivs i en roll där tekniskt hantverk möter strukturerat säkerhetsarbete. Du är trygg i dialogen med både tekniska team och verksamheten och har förmågan att göra komplexa säkerhetsfrågor begripliga.
   

  

 Du har flera års praktisk erfarenhet inom operativ IT-säkerhet och gillar en miljö där du både arbetar hands-on och driver långsiktiga förbättringar.
   

  

* Du har en relevant eftergymnasial utbildning, exempelvis akademisk examen eller YH-utbildning, eller motsvarande erfarenhet.
* Du har minst 4 års erfarenhet inom operativ IT-säkerhet eller liknande.
* Du har mycket goda kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift
* Du har praktisk erfarenhet av sårbarhetstestning, penetrationstestning och relevanta verktyg.
* Du har god förståelse för säkerhet i utvecklingscykeln.
* Du har erfarenhet av härdning av operativsystem, infrastrukturella komponenter och liknande katalogtjänster.
* Du har erfarenhet av kryptografi och kunskap om PKI.
* Du har förmågan att omsätta krav till konkreta säkerhetsåtgärder och tekniska lösningar.
* Du har kännedom om standards inom systematisk informationssäkerhet som t.ex. ISO 27001 eller liknande


**Vi erbjuder...**
* Friskvårdsbidrag: 5000 kr per år.
* Personalrabatt: Förmånliga priser på många av våra produkter.
* Flexibla helgdagar: Möjlighet att byta arbetsfria helgdagar mot andra dagar på året.
* Förmånsportal (Benify): Erbjudanden inom bland annat kollektivtrafik, leasingbil, gym och hälsobehandlingar.
* Trygga försäkringar: Förstärkt föräldrapenning, tjänstepension (ITP) och kompletterande försäkringar.


**Om oss**
 Apoteket grundades 1971 och ägs av svenska staten, vilket innebär att vi arbetar både ansvarsfullt och långsiktigt. Vårt viktiga samhällsuppdrag handlar om att förbättra människors hälsa, läkemedelsanvändning och bidra till ökad patientsäkerhet. Med omkring 400 apotek och 3000 medarbetare över hela landet har vi nära samarbete med hälso- och sjukvården – alltid med omtanke om både människor och miljö.
   

  

 Vårt mål är tydligt: Ett liv i hälsa, för alla.
   

  

  

**Praktisk information:**
 Nyfiken? Det räcker gott.
   

  

 Hör av dig om rollen fångar ditt intresse! Vi berättar gärna mer om tjänsten och vår verksamhet – så du får en tydligare bild och kan avgöra om det känns helt rätt. Vänta bara inte för länge, urval och intervju sker löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum.
   

  

 Skicka ditt CV tillsammans med svar på våra urvalsfrågor. Du behöver inte skriva ett personligt brev – frågorna är utformade för att hjälpa oss förstå mer om dina erfarenheter och kompetenser för matchning mot tjänst.
   

  

 Vi använder tester för objektivt urval som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess. Provanställning tillämpas.
   

  

 Välkommen till Apoteket!

title: Floor host/Receptionist
description:
**Vi på ISS söker en engagerad och positiv Floor Host/Receptionist som brinner för service  - perfekt för dig som vill samla erfarenhet! Vill du vara med och leverera service i världsklass? Då tycker vi att du ska söka denna tjänst!**




**Om rollen**




 Är du den som alltid får folk att känna sig välkomna? Perfekt – då vill vi ha dig!
   

  





 Hos oss blir du Manager of First Impressions på vårt nya huvudkontor i Stockholm. Ena stunden är du barista, nästa gång fixar du event, därefter löser du problem med tekniken. Du kommer alltså arbeta med många och varierande arbetsuppgifter i olika miljöer, och därmed lägger vi stor vikt vid ditt personliga och prestigelösa engagemang samt din förmåga att uppmärksamma och lösa uppkomna situationer. För rollen krävs även att du har god samarbetsförmåga då vi arbetar som ett team.Det här är jobbet för dig som:
   

  




* Har gått gymnasiet och jobbat 1–3 år inom service/event
* Är social, lösnings orienterad och gillar initiativ
* Vill kavla upp ärmarna och lära dig nya saker



**Vem söker vi?**




 Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet från något eller några av dessa yrken: receptionist, konferensvärd, hotell, vaktmästeri, butik eller annat yrke med högt ställda servicekrav. Därutöver har du datorvana och gymnasieutbildning. Erfarenhet av hantering av IT- eller vaktmästeriuppgifter är meriterande.
   

  





 Som person är du serviceinriktad, lyhörd, ansvarsfull och initiativtagande. Du trivs i rollen som företagsrepresentant och delar ISS vilja att leverera service i världsklass. Du har en god kommunikativ förmåga och kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska (och engelska/ alt behärskar engelska) i såväl tal som skrift.
   

  





 ISS värderingar är kvalitet, ansvar, ärlighet, entreprenörskap och gemenskap. Dessa värderingar kommer att vara fundamentala i hur vi arbetar. Därför är det viktigt att du känner att du står för ett arbetssätt och ett förhållningssätt som är i enlighet med dessa. 
   

  





**Vi erbjuder**




 Du kommer att arbeta nära kompetenta medarbetare som du får stort utbyte av, då vi värnar om att hjälpa och utveckla varandra. ISS storlek och breda tjänsteutbud bidrar till att det finns goda utvecklingsmöjligheter såväl nationellt som globalt. Vårt fokus ligger i att ta hand om och utveckla våra medarbetare, för vi tror på att ”People make places”. ISS erbjuder bland annat utbildningar och utvecklingsmöjligheter – allt för att du ska engageras och växa med ISS på lång sikt.
   

  





 Vi värderar mångfald och inkludering högt. Hos ISS är alla människor lika mycket värda och har samma rättigheter och möjligheter. Våra medarbetare speglar samhällets mångfald, vilket både är en konkurrensfördel och en tillgång i vårt långsiktiga hållbara arbete. Vår inkluderande företagskultur stärker våra medarbetare, gör oss mer kreativa och produktiva samt skapar en attraktivare arbetsplats.
   

  





 ISS värderingar, som är fundamentala i hur vi arbetar, är kvalitet, ansvar, ärlighet, entreprenörskap och gemenskap. Därför är det viktigt att du känner att du står för ett arbetssätt och ett förhållningssätt som är i enlighet med dessa.
   

  





**Din ansökan**
  
   

  





 Observera! Vi tar enbart emot ansökningar via vårt rekryteringssystem. Du ansöker genom att gå in på vår hemsida www.se.issworld.com /jobba hos oss/se våra lediga jobb och registrera ditt CV. Ansökningar som skickas på annat sätt kommer inte att kunna besvaras eller hanteras för den här rekryteringen.
   

  





 Har du frågor om tjänsten kontakta rekryterande chef Anders Kling, anders.kling@se.issworld.com

title: Test Automation Engineer - Shape the Future of Corporate Payments | SEB Kort, Solna
description:
SEB Kort is looking for an experienced, technically strong, and motivated Test Automation Engineer. You are passionate about ensuring software quality and thrive on designing robust test frameworks while working in the area of data enhancement. Joining our dynamic international team spread across multiple countries, you will play a key role in delivering reliable, high-quality solutions and supporting our mission to be the market leader for corporate payments in Europe.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Test Automation Engineer, you will be responsible for ensuring the quality and reliability of our software solutions through both automated and manual testing. You will design, develop, and maintain automated test frameworks and scripts while also performing functional and exploratory testing. Working in a cross-functional international agile team you will help drive quality assurance best practices and contribute to building robust, scalable systems.
   

  

**Who We Are Looking For**
 You are a detail-oriented professional with a strong passion for quality and automation. You combine analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a team environment. Clear and effective communication comes naturally to you, enabling smooth interaction with cross-functional teams.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Will Include**
* Working within a team focused on refining and improving data quality, merging incoming data flows, and generating accurate outputs for invoicing
* Performing manual and exploratory testing to ensure comprehensive coverage.
* Designing and implementing automated test scripts for functional, regression, and integration testing.
* Maintaining and improving existing test automation frameworks.
* Collaborating with development teams to integrate automated tests into CI/CD pipelines.
* Identifying, analyzing, and reporting defects, ensuring timely resolution.
* Promoting quality standards within and across teams.


**Who Succeed In This Role We Believe You Have**
* Successfully completed academic studies in IT or a comparable education
* Extensive experience in quality assurance methods (ISTQB certification is an advantage)
* Practical experience in the field of test automation, agile practices, DevOps and similar methods
* Proficiency in Java (senior level) and solid understanding of object-oriented programming.
* Hands-on experience with BDD frameworks such as Cucumber and writing scenarios in Gherkin.
* Familiarity with GitLab for version control and Jenkins for CI/CD pipelines.
* Knowledge of big data technologies including Hadoop, Kafka, and HBase.
* Ability to design and maintain automated test frameworks and integrate them into complex data-driven environments.
* Strong analytical skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving.
* Fluency in English


**What We Offer**
* Agile and modern ways of working
* A flat hierarchy and openness to share ideas, opinions and points of views
* Extensive training and learning opportunities
* Excellent office environment
* Work-life balance


**Ready to take the next step on your career journey?**
 Welcome to our inclusive culture, where our shared values inspire and uplift our team. We celebrate diversity and strive to ensure every employee feels seen, heard, and valued.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today, but no later than 10th of March, 2026. If you have questions about the position, please contact Elinor Franzén, Talent Acquisition Partner, at elinor.franzen@seb.se.
   

  

*SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment.*
*Learn more about working at SEB here:* 
 www.sebgroup.com/techcareers
   

  

 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
**Are you ready to be the glue that holds our dynamic team together? At Alleima, every role plays a vital part in shaping our journey. As an Executive Assistant & Office Coordinator, you will be at the heart of our operations, ensuring everything runs smoothly and efficiently. You will be the assistant for the EVP and Head of Human Resources and EVP & Head of Strategy, M&A and Group IT.**
**Join us and send in your application today!**
**Placement: Stockholm**
**About The Role**
 In this position, you will support our HR and strategy functions while ensuring the daily operations of our Stockholm office run smoothly. You will play an important role in driving digital initiatives and continuous improvements across administrative and internal processes.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Act as a key support function for the managers by proactively handling both administrative and operational tasks, enabling others to perform at their best.
* Take full ownership of calendars, emails, and appointments, ensuring efficient planning, smooth communication, and well-structured workflows.
* Work closely with management on strategic and organizational initiatives, contributing to smart structures and impactful execution
* Own and shape the role as Office Manager for our Stockholm office, ensuring a professional, welcoming, and high-performing workplace
* Drive digitalization and continuous improvements, identifying smarter ways of working and turning ideas into action
* Manage and develop relationships with suppliers, office services, and internal stakeholders, keeping daily operations seamless
* Deliver high-quality administrative support and internal services by effectively planning and coordinating travel, offsites, and workshops, acting as a reliable go-to person who gets things done.


**Your Profile**
 You have a completed upper secondary education (high school diploma) and previous experience from a similar role, such as Executive Assistant and/or Office Coordinator. Do you have a degree from a university will this be seen as a plus. You thrive in a broad and dynamic position where structure, coordination, and attention to detail are essential. With a strong interest in digital tools, systems, and continuous improvement, you are motivated by creating efficient, well-functioning processes and adding value in everyday operations.
   

  

 Your personality is important for this role. As a person, you are a proactive problem-solver who takes initiative and responsibility. You are a proactive and service-minded professional with high integrity, trusted to handle sensitive matters while making things run smoothly. You are structured, solution-focused, and reliable, comfortable working independently while also collaborating closely with others. You communicate with confidence and clarity and are fluent in both Swedish and English, spoken and written, enabling you to interact seamlessly across the organization.
   

  

**What You Can Expect From Us**
 —A culture that values transparency, inclusivity, and sustainability, guided by our deep Swedish roots.
   

  

 —Ample opportunities for learning, experimentation, and development, reflecting our commitment to our team's growth.
   

  

**—Competitive Compensation And Benefits Package.**
**Other information**
**For more information and questions about the position or regarding the recruitment process, please contact:**
 Sara Kühner, Recruiter in Charge, +46 76 495 02 40
   

  

**Union Contacts**
 Maria Sundqvist, Akademikerföreningen, 070 651 73 81
   

  

 Kjell-Åke Klockervold, Ledarna, 070 314 24 43
   

  

 Mikael Larsson, Unionen, 070 307 30 48
   

  

**You are welcome to submit your application no later than 2026-03-08**
**At Alleima, our mission is much more than delivering high-quality products, technology and processes - through collaboration, we develop the best solutions according to our customers' needs and that is how we achieve our business goals is the best way to describe our daily work. With curious employees and safety as our first priority, we create a work environment where you can develop both as a person and in your work. With a clear direction for our journey, where we leverage our position as a technology leader, progressive business partner and where we are a driver in terms of sustainability, we aim to become an even stronger company in our industry. Are you ready to take on this challenge with us? Join us on our journey! www.alleima.com**

title: Information Security Officer
description:
*Vill du ha en central roll inom informationssäkerhet och arbeta nära verksamheten? EP Bank söker nu en Information Security Officer som vill vidareutveckla och stärka bankens säkerhetsarbete.* 




  





**Om EP Bank** 




 EP Bank är en fristående, serviceinriktad och privatägd bank som erbjuder fastighetsfinansiering, rörelse- och förvärvsfinansiering, kredit- och finansieringssamarbeten samt sparkonton. EP Bank har ett 40-tal anställda och kontor i Stockholm. Deras affärskultur bygger på en övertygelse om att långsiktig framgång för banken endast kan vara grundad på framgång för våra kunder och uppdragsgivare.
 



  





**Om Rollen** 




 Som ISO ansvarar du för att vidareutveckla och förvalta bankens arbete inom informationssäkerhet. Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med verksamhet, IT och ledning, där du driver säkerhetsfrågor både strategiskt och operativt.
 



  





 I rollen kommer du att vidareutveckla och säkerställa arbetet med styrdokument och kontinuitetshantering. Du arbetar aktivt med frågor kopplade till DORA samt uppföljning av outsourcade och verksamhetskritiska leverantörer. Rollen är bred och passar dig som trivs med att ta ansvar och arbeta med varierande arbetsuppgifter.
 



  





**Vidare ansvarsområden:** 



* Förvalta och vidareutveckla bankens kontrollramverk
* Genomföra hotbildsanalyser och omvärldsbevakning
* Driva och vidareutveckla incidenthanteringsprocessen
* Förvalta bankens tillgångsregister
* Ansvara för säkerhetsrapportering och KPI:er till styrelse (löpande och årligen)
* Driva och följa upp Security Awareness-insatser



  





**Om Dig** 




 Vi söker dig som är självgående och trivs med att ta ansvar och driva frågor framåt. Du arbetar strukturerat och är van att kombinera strategiskt och operativt arbete inom informationssäkerhet. Du är trygg i regulatoriska krav och har förmåga att tolka och omsätta nya regelverk i praktiken.
 



  





 Som person är du kommunikativ och prestigelös, och trivs i dialog med både verksamhet och ledning. Du uppskattar att arbeta i en föränderlig miljö där du har möjlighet att påverka och driva säkerhetsarbetet framåt
 



  





**Relevant bakgrund:** 



* 3+ års erfarenhet av systematiskt informationssäkerhetsarbete inom liknande verksamhet
* Erfarenhet av leverantörs- och outsourcinghantering i reglerad verksamhet
* God kunskap om relevanta regelverk och standarder, såsom DORA, ISO 27001 och NIST
* Erfarenhet av arbete med regulatoriska krav kopplade till informationssäkerhet och personuppgiftshantering
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift
* Högskoleutbildning inom IT, informationssäkerhet eller motsvarande



  





*EP Bank samarbetar tillsammans med Safemind som rekryteringspartner för denna rekryteringsprocess.*

title: Category Manager
description:
Mar 3, 2026
   

  

 Category Manager
   

  

**About Barilla**
 At Barilla, we believe that every great journey begins with passion and a bold vision. From our humble beginnings in 1877 in a small shop in Parma, Italy, we’ve blossomed into a global leader in the food industry, guided by our commitment to progress and excellence.
   

  

 Today, Barilla is an international group present in more than 100 countries and we are proud to be recognized as the world’s top food company by reputation. With 20 beloved brands in our portfolio, we are world leader for pasta, the number one choice for ready-made sauces in Europe, leader for bakery products in Italy, and for crispbread across Scandinavia with Wasa.
   

  

 Our success story is written by a passionate team of 9,000 dedicated employees across 30 plants and offices worldwide, all united by a shared mission to nourish the future with purpose and passion.
   

  

**Are you ready to add your unique flavor to our journey?**
**Job Description**
 Are you an analytical Category Manager with high ambitions and experience within in FMCG? Do you want to work in a family own company yet with international width? Now you have the chance!
   

  

 Barilla is now looking for a full time
 **Category Manager** 
 to join the Category team based in Stockholm Sweden.
   

  

 As our Category Manager, you will work together both with internal departments such as KAM, Trade Marketing, Finance and the Sales Force as well as with external customers as an expert to ensure that Barilla is seen as the preferred category partner, allowing the categories to grow. Furthermore, you will be the focal point of RGM and you will have the capability to influence the commercial teams as the sales navigator. You are an expert user in NielsenIQ and to analyze market data at customer and category level. We also work with ICA Self Serve, so a plus if you have previous experience from this area.
   

  

**Key Accountabilities**
**Data analysis and management:**
* Analysis of data and insights management for shopper and category, converting into business recommendations and market/costumer opportunities.
* Continuously analyse our assortment and performance in order to find opportunities for growth, come up with recommendations for our central customers as well as our sales force in order to drive profitable growth
* Prepare and present customer presentations to central customers


**Shopper and category intelligence**
* Ensure shopper and category intelligence to answer relevant business questions
* Continuously observing/screening shopper/consumer/category trends and insights
* Recommend and execute category and shopper studies and present those to central customers as well as internal stakeholders


**Shelf layout and placement**
* Develop and provide shelf placement guidelines and recommendations for improving shelf performance and product initiatives.


**Insights sharing and cross-functional collaboration**
* Work closely with sales, trade marketing, category marketing and leverage key insights to drive sales and shares
* Hold engaging presentations of all category findings/analysis internally as well as during our Sales conferences to our Sales force
* Be the sparring partner and addressing queries related to ongoing projects and initiatives


**Qualifications**
* Great record of accomplishment, with a minimum of 5 years of work experience from category management in the FMCG setting. Any additional experience in Sales and Trade Marketing will be considered a strong advantage
* Previous Experience in managing customer data driven decision making
* Excellent communication skills in Swedish and English
* Expert user of NielsenIQ
* Expert user of all Microsoft office tools .
* Strong people management skills, by driving shared goals, building trust and using an open communication
* Demonstration of leadership and ownership, showing curiosity and creativity, while building strong and trustful relationships
* Project management and analytical skills with a strong “can do” attitude that allows sound decisions making within established guidelines
* International growth mindset, ideally proven with international experiences


**Our Offer**
* High Responsibility: From day one, we entrust you with significant responsibilities. Your impact matters, and we recognize it.
* Personal Development: Join us for professional growth and gain global experience in a dynamic international setting, offering extensive exposure and opportunities to excel.
* Continuous Learning: We believe in growth. Expect regular feedback and training opportunities to maximize your potential.
* Flexible Work Arrangements: Embrace our hybrid approach, and flexible working hours.
* Diversity & Inclusion: Engage in national and international projects that champion diversity and inclusion. Your unique perspective matters here.
* Excellent Public Transportation Access: Whether you prefer biking or public transport, we have got you covered. Enjoy working in a stylish, modern office which ensures easy access to various transportation options.
* Motivated Team: Be part of a dynamic and motivated team. We thrive on collaboration, innovation, and mutual support.
* Positive and Inclusive Culture: Experience a workplace where positivity and inclusivity are at the core. Our flat hierarchies encourage open communication and mutual respect.


**Application**
 Candidates will be contacted by our international Recruiter at Talent Solutions, part of ManpowerGroup. Our Recruiter is based in Germany, for this reason, a number with the country code +49 will be used.
   

  

 Applications and CVs are sent via our job portal www.barillacareers.com Join us / Current Job Opportunities. If you have any questions regarding the recruitment process, please get in touch with Talent Acquisition, Auday Alkhatib - auday.alkhatib@external.barilla.com
   

  

 We are reviewing candidates regularly so do not wait with your application!
   

  

 At Barilla, we are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace where diversity in all its forms is valued and embraced. Our employment policies and practices are designed to ensure equal employment opportunities for all, regardless of age, race, color, citizenship, faith, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, gender identity or characteristics of expression, sexual orientation, marital status, genetic information, medical condition, protected veteran status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law. The Swedish organizations is the first LGBTQI-certified company in the food industry and our commitment to equal employment stems from our unwavering belief that it is not only the right thing to do, but it is also a fundamental driver of innovation and business success.
   

  

 Trade Marketing & Sales
   

  

 Sweden
   

  

 Stockholm
   

  

 Permanent

title: Front Office Specialist
description:
FactSet creates flexible, open data and software solutions for over 200,000 investment professionals worldwide, providing instant access to financial data and analytics that investors use to make crucial decisions.
   

  

 At FactSet, our values are the foundation of everything we do. They express how we act and operate, serve as a compass in our decision-making, and play a big role in how we treat each other, our clients, and our communities. We believe that the best ideas can come from anyone, anywhere, at any time, and that curiosity is the key to anticipating our clients’ needs and exceeding their expectations.
   

  

 FactSet’s Front Office Solutions Sales team empowers investment professionals with innovative solutions and industry expertise. Based in our Stockholm office, you’ll be part of a collaborative local team deeply engaged with clients and global colleagues, dedicated to driving value and delivering differentiated experiences. Our environment is dynamic, inclusive, and focused on professional development, making it a supportive space for high-impact client engagement and growth.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 As a Front Office Solutions Sales Specialist located in Stockholm, you will be responsible for nurturing, expanding, and maximizing FactSet’s presence amongst our Nordics and DACH client base.
   

  

* Develop and maintain strong relationships with asset managers, asset owners, and other investment professionals throughout Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Germany, Austria and Switzerland.
* Leverage your expertise to provide insights into clients’ businesses and workflows, engaging in meaningful, two-way conversations to solve challenges and drive client differentiation.
* Tailor communication and proposals to align with client value drivers unique to the Nordic region.
* Own and guide complex sales cycles from initial engagement and product demonstration through negotiation and close, confidently discussing commercial terms.
* Collaborate locally and globally with sales enablement, product marketing, consulting, and business stakeholders to promote FactSet’s Front Office Solutions and identify new business opportunities.
* Become a trusted subject matter expert for FactSet in the Nordics, gathering market intelligence, positioning FactSet’s competitive strengths, and relaying actionable feedback to influence our product strategy.
* Record and analyze client interactions and sales activities in CRM; facilitate feedback between sales and the Buy-Side Front Office Product team.
* Be accountable for your book of business in the region, driving growth across products and workflows and ensuring excellent client satisfaction.


**What We’re Looking For**
* Proven success managing complex sales cycles, 10 years or relevant experience supporting clients in financial services or investment management within the Nordics.
* Academic Excellence: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or related discipline.
* Excellent relationship-building and communication skills, with the ability to teach, tailor messaging, and negotiate with senior client stakeholders- Commercial acumen and confidence in driving conversations about value, pricing, and deal closure.
* Eager to become a local subject matter expert, understanding both client and competitor landscapes relevant to the Nordics and DACH markets.
* Strong collaborative approach, comfortable working with cross-functional teams in a global organization.
* Self-starter, highly organized, accountable for managing a regional book of business and reporting on progress.
* Fluency in English is required; fluency in a Nordic language is highly beneficial.
* Experience using CRM systems to track client engagement and sales activities.


**What's In It For You**
 At FactSet, our people are our greatest asset, and our culture is our biggest competitive advantage. Being a FactSetter means:
   

  

* The opportunity to join an S&P 500 company with over 45 years of sustainable growth powered by the entrepreneurial spirit of a start-up.
* Support for your total well-being. This includes health, life, and disability insurance, as well as retirement savings plans and a discounted employee stock purchase program, plus paid time off for holidays, family leave, and company-wide wellness days.
* Flexible work accommodations. We value work/life harmony and offer our employees a range of accommodations to help them achieve success both at work and in their personal lives.
* A global community dedicated to volunteerism and sustainability, where collaboration is always encouraged, and individuality drives solutions.
* Career progression planning with dedicated time each month for learning and development.
* Business Resource Groups open to all employees that serve as a catalyst for connection, growth, and belonging.


**Company Overview**
 Learn more about our benefits here.
   

  

 FactSet (NYSE:FDS | NASDAQ:FDS) helps the financial community to see more, think bigger, and work better. Our digital platform and enterprise solutions deliver financial data, analytics, and open technology to more than 8,200 global clients, including over 200,000 individual users. Clients across the buy-side and sell-side, as well as wealth managers, private equity firms, and corporations, achieve more every day with our comprehensive and connected content, flexible next-generation workflow solutions, and client-centric specialized support. As a member of the S&P 500, we are committed to sustainable growth and have been recognized among the Best Places to Work in 2023 by Glassdoor as a Glassdoor Employees’ Choice Award winner. Learn more at www.factset.com and follow us on X and LinkedIn.
   

  

 At FactSet, we celebrate difference of thought, experience, and perspective. Qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to characteristics protected by law.

title: Supply Chain Management Consultant (D365FO)
description:
Wipro Limited is a leading technology services and consulting company focused on building innovative solutions that address clients’ most complex digital transformation needs. Leveraging our holistic portfolio of capabilities in consulting, design, engineering, and operations, we help clients realize their boldest ambitions and build future-ready, sustainable businesses. With over 230,000 employees and business partners across 65 countries, we deliver on the promise of helping our clients, colleagues, and communities thrive in an ever-changing world.
 



  





**Role Overview:** 




  





**Role: SCM D365FO Consultant** 




**Location: Tumba, Stockholm** 




**Work Setting: Hybrid** 




  





 We are seeking a highly skilled D365FO Supply Chain Management (SCM) Consultant to join our dynamic team at Wipro. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO) with a focus on Supply Chain Management (SCM) modules. This role involves working closely with clients to design, implement, and optimize end-to-end supply chain processes using D365FO.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities:** 




  




* Lead and participate in D365FO SCM implementations, including requirement gathering, solution design, and deployment.
* Configure and customize SCM modules such as Procurement & Sourcing, Inventory & Warehouse Management, Product Information Management, and Transportation Management.
* Conduct gap analysis and recommend best practices to optimize supply chain processes.
* Work with cross-functional teams including Finance, Manufacturing, and IT to ensure seamless integration.
* Provide end-user training and post-implementation support.
* Collaborate with technical teams for system customizations and integrations.
* Stay updated with latest D365FO updates and supply chain trends to enhance solution offerings.



  





**Required Skills & Qualifications:** 




  




* 5+ years of experience in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365FO) with expertise in Supply Chain Management (SCM).
* Strong knowledge of procurement, inventory, warehouse, demand planning, logistics, and production control processes.
* Experience in end-to-end implementations, rollouts, and upgrades of D365FO SCM.
* Understanding of Power Platform, Power BI, and integrations with third-party systems.
* Ability to translate business requirements into D365FO solutions.
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Microsoft Certifications in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management (preferred).



  





**Preferred Qualifications:** 




  




* Experience working with global clients and multi-country implementations.
* Knowledge of Azure DevOps, LCS (Lifecycle Services), and Agile methodologies.
* Understanding of finance and manufacturing modules in D365FO.



  





**Why Join Wipro?** 




  




* Work on global enterprise projects with cutting-edge technologies.
* Opportunity for career growth and learning in Microsoft Dynamics.
* Be part of a diverse and inclusive work culture.
* Competitive compensation and benefits package.



  





  





**Diversity and Inclusion at Wipro** 




  





*We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, marital status, age, ethnic and national origin, sexual and political orientation and disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law.* 




  





 Reinvent your world. We are building a modern Wipro. We are an end-to-end digital transformation partner with the boldest ambitions. To realize them, we need people inspired by reinvention. Of yourself, your career, and your skills. We want to see the constant evolution of our business and our industry. It has always been in our DNA - as the world around us changes, so do we. Join a business powered by purpose and a place that empowers you to design your own reinvention. Come to Wipro. Realize your ambitions. Applications from people with disabilities are explicitly welcome.

title: Sales Development Associate
description:
Location: Kista, SE
 



  





**Role Purpose** 




 The Sales Development Associate is a key daily resource for clients, ensuring the smooth operational execution of client requests and sales activities. This role is vital in building foundational relationships and driving client satisfaction by acting as an internal advocate and ensuring timely, accurate service delivery. The SDA supports the identification and nurturing of cross-sell and upsell opportunities, demonstrating a client-centric mindset through high levels of accountability. The role’s key focus is to enable additional selling time for the seller(s) they are supporting.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Support the identification, qualification and closure of sales opportunities, driving conversion from leads to close particularly for low complexity technology sales, cross-selling, and up-selling.
* Act as a client Subject Matter Expert (SME) providing extended support to specialist business areas e.g., Microsoft EAs, cloud.
* Triage renewals for existing accounts to upsell opportunities and support client retention, collaborating with the Account Executive and producing quotes through to sales order placement.
* Promote online/Global catalogues, manage additions of parts as clients require them and maintain End Of Life products.
* Contribute to sales pitches, bids, deal registration and tenders under guidance.
* Contribute to the account plan (where applicable) under guidance from the account plan owner.
* Contribute to pipeline coverage and growth across accounts.
* Become the client's primary point of contact for support, serving as the 'emotional glue' that ensures customers feel acknowledged and valued.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with multiple stakeholders and be seen as a dependable and trustworthy resource who inspires confidence in others by proactively taking ownership through clear communication.
* Improve customer satisfaction and assist in escalations.
* Ensure adherence to standardized processes (e.g., tiered qualification, procurement rules, framework compliance, escalation paths).
* Proactively collaborate with internal departments for a coordinated, client-focused approach to account growth and to ensure all client requests are picked up within relevant timescales (e.g., Client Operations, Partnerships, Purchasing, POSS etc).
* Utilize internal CRM systems to produce timely and accurate forecasts, pipeline data and maintain client contacts.



  





**Key skills and experience required:** 




**Sales Cycle Management:** 



* Basic experience with the sales cycle
* Requires coaching support for managing complex cycles



**Stakeholder Management:** 



* Foundational ability to engage stakeholders
* Communicates insightful ideas and maintains relationships



**Commercial Acumen:** 



* Developing understanding of the business environment
* Aware of external factors impacting performance



**Technology Business Acumen:** 



* Foundational grasp of financial levers and industry trends
* Understands how technology impacts business decisions



**Communication:** 



* Foundational communication skills
* Adapts style to suit audience and situation



  





**About Insight:** 




 We believe that by giving you the freedom to think big and empower you to reach your full potential, together we will achieve the best outcomes. Along with excellent benefits and a compelling reward package, we offer the opportunity to work in a supportive environment with a high level of autonomy and creativity - there’s a reason our average employee tenure is over 6 years.
 



 We strive to display our three core values of Hunger, Heart, and Harmony every day. They represent and drive who we are here at Insight and by doing so we are doing amazing things. Insight started in a garage in 1988 and it is through harnessing our three core values that two brothers, Eric and Tim Crown, steered Insight to the Fortune 500 company it is today. We are now a Global IT Services and Solutions business, passionate about helping customers and the real people who sit behind them.
 



**Application Details:** 




 Insight is an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to achieving diversity and equality within our organisation. We seek out people from diverse backgrounds and encourage you to apply.
 



 We will endeavour to contact you within five business days, should we feel your profile is a good match for this role. If you do not hear from us within this timeframe, please presume that on this occasion, your application was not successful.

title: Områdesansvarig informationssäkerhet till Statens inköpscentral
description:
*Har du arbetat strategiskt med informationssäkerhetsfrågor, kanske i offentlig sektor, och vill bidra till att utveckla kravställningen i de statliga ramavtalen och i de kontrakt som avropas från dem? Tycker du om att samarbeta och hitta konkreta och praktiska lösningar där olika perspektiv behöver balanseras?*
*På Statens inköpscentral får du möjlighet att arbeta med komplexa och samhällsviktiga uppgifter i en kunskapsintensiv verksamhet där du tar ansvar och bidrar med din expertkompetens. Många offentligt styrda verksamheter använder våra ramavtal och vi har en central roll i upphandlingssverige. Vårt mål är att skapa de bästa möjliga förutsättningarna för dem som använder våra ramavtal - och vi har roligt när vi gör det!*
 Om oss
   

  

 Statens inköpscentral vid Kammarkollegiet ansvarar för den statliga inköpssamordningen genom att erbjuda ramavtal inom allt från IT och konsulttjänster till kontorsmateriel och resor. Verksamheten ska vara behovsdriven och affärsmässig, med målet att effektivisera myndigheters försörjning och skapa attraktiva affärer för leverantörer. Vi arbetar idag med fem fokusområden: e-handel, försörjningstrygghet, hållbarhet, tillgänglighet och informationssäkerhet. Den tjänst som vi nu rekryterar till kommer att vara områdesansvarig för området informationssäkerhet.
   

  

 Varför söker vi dig?
   

  

 Vi vidareutvecklar och fördjupar vårt arbete inom säkerhetsområdet och erbjuder nu en heltidstjänst som får ett samlat ansvar för säkerhetsfrågor i våra upphandlingar och ramavtal – i bred mening. Ditt huvudfokus kommer vara frågor kopplat till informationssäkerhet men du kommer även arbeta med säkerhetsfrågor i ett vidare perspektiv exempelvis säkerhetsskyddad upphandling (SUA), cybersäkerhet, sekretess, riskhantering och övergripande säkerhetsstyrning kopplat till upphandling och kontrakt. Du får ett stort eget mandat att utforma och vidareutveckla hur Statens inköpscentral ska arbeta med säkerhetsfrågor i framtiden.
   

  

 Huvudansvaret för GDPR-frågor ligger inte i rollen, men du förväntas kunna identifiera gränsdragningar och samarbeta tätt med kollegor inom området. Arbetet innebär även generellt ett nära samarbete med kollegor för att utveckla och följa upp våra ramavtal. Denna tjänst kommer vara placerad på avdelningens stabsfunktion tillsammans med andra för avdelningen gemensamma kompetenser och din närmaste chef kommer vara avdelningschefen. På avdelningen arbetar idag drygt 70 medarbetare. Du blir en del av en kompetent och engagerad arbetsplats där ansvar, trivsel och samarbete står högt. Vi har låg personalomsättning och höga frisktal.
   

  

 Arbetsbeskrivning
   

  

 Upphandlingar och förstudier genomförs i projektform tillsammans med dina kollegor inom avdelningen. Din roll blir att
   

  

* agera bollplank och vara ett stöd i kravställning kopplad till säkerhetsområdet och där bistå i att göra analyser och behovsbedömningar
* vara ett stöd i förvaltningen av ramavtalen och uppföljningen av ramavtal och leverantörer inom våra ramavtalsområden
* genomföra omvärldsbevakning, marknadsanalys och riskbedömningar
* säkerställa att nationella riktlinjer och EU-krav inom säkerhetsområdet, ex. cybersäkerhet och säkerhetsskydd, omsätts till praktiska och relevanta krav i upphandlingar
* följa utvecklingen inom lagstiftning relevant för området
* samverka med expertmyndigheter och andra nationella aktörer med uppdrag inom området
* presentera och förklara komplexa säkerhetsfrågor för både interna och externa grupper


 Det här är en ny tjänst inom avdelningen, där arbetet organiserats och bedrivits på annat sätt tidigare, och du kommer därför få goda möjligheter att själv bidra till hur rollen vidareutvecklas framåt. Rollen ställer höga på självständighet och du förväntas besitta en god strategisk förmåga men också drivet att tillsammans med dina kollegor omsätta den i praktiken till attraktiva och användarvänliga ramavtal.
   

  

 Är du vår framtida kollega?
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har
   

  

* Akademisk utbildning inom informationssäkerhet, IT, juridik eller annan utbildning som bedöms relevant för rollen
* Flera års relevant och aktuell erfarenhet av strategiskt arbete med informationssäkerhet, exempelvis informationssäkerhetsstyrning och riskbedömning
* Erfarenhet av att formulera och omsätta informationssäkerhetskrav i styrande dokument, avtal eller upphandling
* Erfarenhet från offentlig sektor eller annan reglerad verksamhet
* Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig skriftligt på svenska


 Meriterande för tjänsten är
   

  

* Kunskap om relevanta standarder och ramverk, exempelvis ISO 27001
* Erfarenhet av säkerhetsskyddsarbete och/eller arbete med säkerhetsskyddsavtal (SUA)
* Certifieringar inom informationssäkerhet (t.ex. CISSP, CISM, CRISC, CISA)
* Kunskap om aktuella regelverk inom cybersäkerhet och leverantörsrisker


 Som områdesansvarig
   

  

* håller du god struktur och tidplaner
* skiftar du fokus mellan olika upphandlingsområden vid behov
* balanserar du tekniska, juridiska och affärsmässiga perspektiv
* gör du nödvändiga prioriteringar och avvägningar
* presenterar du tryggt och pedagogiskt inför grupper
* samarbetar du väl och bidrar till engagemang och delaktighet


 Du trivs i en roll där du kombinerar analys, rådgivning och praktisk problemlösning – och där du får vara drivkraften i att stärka säkerhetsarbetet i staten.
   

  

 Mer information
   

  

 Tillträde snarast. Anställningen är tillsvidare som inleds med sex månaders provanställning. Tjänsten kan komma att omfattas av säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen.
   

  

 Från den 1 januari 2027 blir Statens inköpscentral en del av Upphandlingsmyndigheten
   

  

 Det är en unik chans att arbeta i Sveriges mest kompetenta miljö för upphandling och juridik, med oförändrat uppdrag men ännu större möjligheter att påverka framtidens offentliga affärer.
   

  

 Kontaktpersoner
   

  

 Om du har några frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Anna Clara Wittgren, avdelningschef, telefon 08-700 06 48, Anna.Clara.Wittgren@Kammarkollegiet.se
   

  

 Fackliga företrädare
   

  

 Karin Edstrand, SACO-S, telefon 08 - 700 08 48
   

  

 Lisa Ericsson, ST, telefon 08 – 700 06 40
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Låter det intressant? Välkommen med din ansökan via www.kammarkollegiet.se/jobba-har, inklusive CV och personligt brev, senast den 30/3

title: Customer Success Manager
description:
At Billogram we like to be the good guys of billing, making money when clients are happy and pay on time rather than from missed payments. We believe it always pays off to treat people well. That’s why our platform for invoices and payments is built to remove pain and instead be of gain to both our clients and their customers – improving their relations.
   

  

 To ensure continuous growth and become a leader in the European consumer billing market we are looking for a Customer Success Manager to join our team.
   

  

 About the role:
   

  

 As Customer Success Manager, your primary responsibility is to act as a trusted strategic advisor to our clients. You'll drive profitability and customer retention by ensuring clients achieve their desired outcomes with our product. You will serve as the main point of contact for a number of large and mid-sized clients, gaining a deep understanding of their business goals and challenges.
   

  

 In this role, you are the one who connects the dots. You zoom out to see the big picture and ensure that every decision made along the way supports the long-term commercial success of the partnership. You aren't afraid to take charge, drive your own agenda, and lead both internal and external stakeholders to ensure we deliver real value.
   

  

 Primary responsibilities:
   

  

* Strategic Account Ownership: You develop and drive the client agenda, creating account plans with specific goals and breaking them down into impactful activities.
* Commercial Drive: You focus on maximizing client lifetime value by identifying business opportunities and leading complex contract negotiations, often at C-level.
* Stakeholder Management: You act as a cross-functional leader, coordinating efforts across different teams at Billogram to ensure the client journey is successful.
* Influence Product Development: You act as a strategic bridge between the market and our product team, providing crucial feedback to ensure our roadmap aligns with high-impact client needs.
* Drive Process Improvement: You take the lead on developing and implementing your own initiatives to improve and streamline processes and ways of working within the Customer Success team.
* Lead and Escalate: You have the ability to classify issues and the confidence to drive them to resolution, escalating when necessary to protect the business and the customer experience.


 What we are looking for:
   

  

 We are looking for a self-starter who is comfortable taking the lead and acting as a clear, guiding voice for our clients. You excel at building trusted relationships and navigating complex stakeholder landscapes with ease. While you are confident in your expertise and able to be a decisive voice in a room, you always remain humble and genuinely curious about the people and businesses you support. You are highly organized and possess a strong ability to zoom out and connect the dots to see the logic and connections in complex environments.
   

  

 What will help you succeed:
   

  

* Proven experience in managing a client portfolio and driving formal account plans and review cycles.
* Experience in initiating and leading complex contract negotiations and renegotiations, ideally at C-level.
* Solid skills in acting as a cross-functional project leader, coordinating complex client journeys and outcomes by leveraging internal teams (e.g., sales, product, tech).
* Industry experience from the payment landscape or our focus verticals (Telecom, Utilities, Insurance) is a plus.
* Experience from working in a start-up/scale-up environment within SaaS is beneficial.
* Bachelor's degree in business administration or equivalent is beneficial. .
* Fluent in Swedish and English, both written and spoken is a requirement.


 What we offer:
   

  

 At Billogram we promise you an exciting journey as we break into new markets and grow at a rapid pace. That aside you will get lots of freedom and opportunity to develop together with great colleagues at our office, right in the center of Stockholm. We also offer flexible working hours, exciting challenges and a whole lot of laughs.
   

  

 We're committed to your long-term development, offering clear career progression paths. You'll also enjoy a fantastic benefits package, including 30 (+2) days of holiday, an occupational pension, a flexible wellness allowance, sick leave top-up, and parental leave compensation. At Billogram, our inclusive and diverse culture ensures that everyone can enjoy a fulfilling career and a great work-life balance.
   

  

*At Billogram, we provide equal opportunities and strive for an inclusive and diverse environment. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to gender, transgender identity or expression, ethnicity, religion or other beliefs, disability, sexual orientation, or age.*
 Welcome to the world of Billogram!

title: Commercial Solution Engineer
description:
Snowflake is about empowering enterprises to achieve their full potential — and people too. With a culture that’s all in on impact, innovation, and collaboration, Snowflake is the sweet spot for building big, moving fast, and taking technology — and careers — to the next level.
   

  

 We are looking for a
 **Commercial Solution Engineer** 
 for the Spanish market who can solve our customer’s most complex problems. In this role you will work directly with the account sales team and channel partners to understand the needs of our customers, strategize on how to best support them in their evaluation and ultimately help our customers choose Snowflake as their solution.
   

  

 As a Snowflake Commercial Solution Engineer you share our passion for solving complex data challenges and helping organizations to get the most out of their data assets. Our technical landscape is ever evolving and you are keen to learn new skills and put them into practice on real world challenges. You are able to translate features and functions into solutions that solve business problems, in conversations with technical or business teams, with end users or executives.
   

  

 In this role you will get to:
   

  

* Present Snowflake’s technology and vision to executives and technical contributors at prospects and customers
* Work hands-on with prospects and customers to demonstrate and communicate the value of Snowflake technology throughout the sales cycle, from demo to proof of concept to design and implementation
* Create and develop technical champions in your accounts to drive deals and achieve a technical win
* Be at the cutting edge of Snowflake technology and confidently present Snowflake roadmap features and functionality to customers and/or prospects
* Immerse and enable yourself in the ever-evolving industry, maintaining a deep understanding of competitive and complementary technologies and vendors and how to position Snowflake in relation to them
* Work closely with other sales engineers to make each other the best and constantly learn from wins and losses
* Collaborate with Product Management, Engineering, and Marketing to continuously improve Snowflake’s products and marketing
* Represent Snowflake at industry or customer events
* Work with our ecosystem and implementation partners to build joint architectures or collaborate on account strategies and initiatives to help our customers be successful


 On day one we will expect you to have:
   

  

* Sales engineering/solution architect experience in a SaaS environment or relevant industry experience (analytics, data science, data engineering etc)
* Outstanding presentation skills to both technical and executive audiences, whether impromptu on a whiteboard or using presentations and demos
* Understanding of and experience with data architecture, data analytics and cloud technology
* Hands on experience with SQL
* Ability to solve customer specific business problems and apply Snowflake’s solutions
* Customer-facing skills to effectively communicate our vision to a wide variety of technical and executive audiences both written and verbal


 Preferred (but not required) to have:
   

  

* Hands on experience with Python
* Experience working with modern data technology (e.g. dbt, spark, containers, devops tooling, orchestration tools, git, etc.)
* Experience with data science and machine learning technology


 People want to buy from people who understand them. Our Solution Engineers build connections, relationships and trust with our customers that last. We love to learn, are open to giving and receiving feedback and are passionate about making our customers and each other successful.
   

  

 Think you have what it takes but not sure that you tick every box above? Apply anyways! We value the broad range of experience our teams bring to the table and believe our customers are more successful because of it.
   

  

 Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.
   

  

 How do you want to make your impact?
   

  

 For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com

title: Product Manager Software & Services to Erinion
description:
Are you passionate about making a sustainable impact in the world of electric transport? Do you thrive in an innovative and entrepreneurial environment? Then you might be the new Product Manager Software & Services to Erinion!
   

  

**About Erinion**
 Erinion designs, builds and operates depot and destination charging solutions for heavy-duty vehicles. As part of the Scania Group, we combine the strength of a global transport leader with the agility of a fast-growing startup company. Our approach focuses on energy efficiency and optimal utilisation, ensuring that electrification is not only sustainable but also financially and commercially viable for our customers. We support different ways of financing, owning and operating charging sites, so that each customer can find the model that best fits their business. With operations across eight European markets and global expansion underway, we enable transport operators to electrify their fleets with confidence and long-term profitability.
   

  

 Your role
   

  

 We are looking for a Product Manager with a strong commercial background and a track record from software or service product development and commercialisation. As Product Manager Software & Services, you will be part of shaping Erinion’s product strategy and our ways of working both within your area and for the entire product organisation. You will be part of small and ambitious product team aiming to re-shape the future of heavy-duty transportation in Europe and beyond, working with hardware, software and services as well as new business models.
   

  

 Your responsibilities
   

  

 You will own the product strategy and roadmap for Erinion’s software and service offering, ensuring the product portfolio meet current and future customer needs across all Erinion markets. You have the responsibility from idea to commercialization and post launch follow ups and improvements. To achieve this, you will work closely with customers, suppliers as well as with colleagues across the Erinion and Scania organisation in tech, operations, purchasing and commercial.
   

  

 You ensure Erinion has the most competitive offer on the market and that it is scalable and robust. You will continuously find new ways to drive product growth. The aim with our product is to drive down total cost of ownership for our customers, so they become more profitable when they go electric, and to enable them to maximize asset utilization. Your job can range from one day setting up an aftermarket service solution across all our markets to the next launching software solutions that increase customer uptime.
   

  

 Your Profile
   

  

 We’re looking for a commercially minded product manager who has successfully built and scaled software or service-based offerings. You combine analytical and strategic thinking with hands-on execution and know how to translate customer needs into products that drive growth and profitability. You enjoy working closely with customers and partners, thrive in a dynamic environment, and help build structure as the organisation grows.
   

  

  

* 3+ years of experience in product management or a similar role, with proven ownership of product roadmaps and execution.
* Strong commercial mindset with experience driving growth and profitability through product development.
* Analytical and strategic, able to identify new business opportunities and turn insights into clear execution plans.
* Comfortable working closely with customers, suppliers, and other stakeholders across countries and cultures.
* Skilled at simplifying complex product offerings and clearly articulating customer and technical needs.
* Hands-on and pragmatic, able to build and launch products both with and without an engineering team.


 Our offer
   

  

 Join our passionate team in Stockholm city, at Drottninggatan 29, and be a driving force in the future of sustainable transportation. At Erinion, you won't just be a part of the change, you'll be pioneering it. We offer a competitive compensation package, alongside unparalleled professional growth opportunities. This is an early-stage company where your ideas are valued, your contributions have a significant impact, and you have the chance to develop and grow alongside Erinion.
   

  

 Further information and application
   

  

 We are driven by our belief that the power of diversity fuels innovation. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. We are working with an ongoing selection; please apply as soon as possible if you are interested, but no later than 2026-03-12. Your application should include your CV and certificate of your highest finished degree. Please note that we cannot accept applications over email. If you have any further questions, contact Sebastian Glaas at sebastian.glaas@andpartners.se. A background check might be conducted for this position
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Acne Studios System Developer
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone's contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimize our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As a System Developer in our Stockholm headquarters, you'll join a skilled development team working at the intersection of fashion, retail, and technology. This role is ideal for someone with 1-2 years of experience in Dynamics 365 F&O and Azure integrations who is ready to take the next step and deepen their expertise in a dynamic, global environment.
   

  

 You'll collaborate closely with senior developers, testers, IT Business Partners, and teams across the company. Together, you'll design, build, and maintain solutions that support our global operations - from purchasing and logistics to retail and finance. Our tech stack includes Dynamics 365 F&O, Azure integration services, and Azure DevOps.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As System Developer your main contribution will be to build and maintain solutions that support our global operations. You will report to the Head of System Development & Business Relations. You will play a vital role in the development team where you will:
   

  

* Develop, test, deploy, and maintain software solutions and integrations that align with our architectural and coding standards
* Grow your craft with coaching from experienced senior developers
