* Proven experience delivering end‑to‑end performance review cycles, including template design, eligibility rules, rating scales, competencies, and workflows
* Strong experience configuring and supporting calibration sessions, including calibration setup, participant security, and facilitation readiness
* Strong proficiency in Advanced Reporting and Calculated Fields, with the ability to build complex, cross-domain reports
* Solid understanding of HCM fundamentals, covering both business processes (Hire, Change Job, Termination) and configuration concepts (condition rules, orgs, hierarchies, custom objects)
* Working knowledge of Workday Security, including constrained/unconstrained roles and segmented security
* Proven analytical and problem-solving skills, with an eye for process efficiency and data integrity
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate business needs into system design
* Bachelor’s degree in HR, IT, Business Systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience


**It's Great if you have:**
* Experience implementing or optimizing continuous performance management, check-ins, or development planning
* Experience supporting multi-country or regionally varied performance frameworks
* Familiarity with testing frameworks, release management, or regression testing
* Experience working in an international or fast-paced environment
* Workday certification(s) in Talent & Performance, or HCM
* Interest in expanding knowledge beyond Workday into other people technology platforms and tools.


**What you'll get:**
* Medical, dental, mental health support line, annual leave, travel discounts, sick days, and the AgodaChoice flexible spending benefit
* Provident fund benefits, and life insurance
* Flexibility to help you work in the way that's best for you with a work from home allowance, 30-day work from anywhere benefit, and hybrid working options
* Unlimited online learning access, several in-person development programs for each stage of your career, and career paths for management and individual contributors
* The chance to live in Bangkok, one of the best cities in Southeast Asia and a central hub for travel. Within 3 hours you can hit any of the most popular countries like Vietnam, Singapore, Laos, Malaysia, and Hong Kong, just to name a few
* A generous relocation package to help you get here comfortably

title: [ITO] (GSD) Workday HCM Specialist (Bangkok-based, relocation provided)
description:

 At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.
   

  

 Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. The GSD team uses state-of-the-art cloud management technologies combined with the best on-site services to manage the latest hardware running Windows, macOS, and Linux.

 As a Workday HCM Specialist, you will serve as the primary expert ensuring our core HCM processes operate seamlessly, with a focus on scaling and enhancing the setup in alignment with organizational requirements. While HCM will be your core area, you will also contribute across other domains and additional HRIS platforms to strengthen our overall People Technology foundation.
   

  

 You’ll partner closely with People and IT teams to deliver scalable solutions, optimize existing processes, and explore new ways Workday and other tools can bring value to the organization.

* Lead, configure, maintain, and support Workday HCM setup including core configuration, organization structures and business process frameworks
* Partner closely with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into scalable Workday solutions that enhance employee experience.

* Own documentation and knowledge sharing, including SOPs, configuration guides, and process maps to strengthen team resiliency.
* Gain opportunities to work across other Workday domains and internal tools, expanding expertise based on project needs and personal interests.
* Stay informed on new Workday releases and people technology trends, identifying opportunities for enhancement and automation.

* Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate business needs into system design.

**It's Great if you have:**

* Knowledge of Workday integrations (EIBs, Core Connectors, Studio)
* Familiarity with HCM rollouts, testing frameworks, release management, or regression testing
* Experience working in an international or fast-paced environment
* Workday certification(s) in HCM or any other modules
* Interest in expanding knowledge beyond Workday into other people technology platforms and tools.


**What you'll get:**
* Medical, dental, mental health support line, annual leave, travel discounts, sick days, and the AgodaChoice flexible spending benefit
* Provident fund benefits, and life insurance
* Flexibility to help you work in the way that's best for you with a work from home allowance, 30-day work from anywhere benefit, and hybrid working options.
* Unlimited online learning access, several in-person development programs for each stage of your career, and career paths for management and individual contributors
* The chance to live in Bangkok, one of the best cities in Southeast Asia and a central hub for travel. Within 3 hours you can hit any of the most popular countries like Vietnam, Singapore, Laos, Malaysia, and Hong Kong, just to name a few.

title: Senior .NET Developer – Stockholm
description:
***Är du en systemutvecklare inom .NET som inte bara skriver kod, utan utvecklar lösningar som fungerar idag såväl som håller och utvecklas över tid? På Solita vill vi också något mer, vi tar initiativ, samarbetar och utforskar för att skapa en AI- och datadriven framtid.***




  





 Som Senior .NET Developer blir du del av ett växande område med runt 20 konsulter inom cloud, AI och systemutveckling. Samtidigt får du kollegor från hela Solita med expertis i allt från data, strategi och mycket mer att dela kunskap och perspektiv med, läs mer om det här och ta del av insikter från utvecklare på Solita redan nu i vår Dev blog här.
 



  





 När vi nu växer gör dina möjligheter också det, du får vara med och påverka kulturen, affären och din roll.
 



  





**En dag på Solita** 




 En dag på Solita kan börja med ett standup-möte med teamet på kunduppdraget, ibland består det av fler kollegor från Solita. Är det fredag kanske du jobbar från Solita kontoret i Stockholm för att starta dagen med frukost. Andra dagar jobbar du från kundens kontor eller hemifrån.
 



  





 Sen kliver du in i ditt arbete, när du stöter på ett problem söker du upp en lång diskussionstråd i slack med insikter från kollegor som haft samma problem. Andra gånger passar du på att fråga en kollega vid kaffemaskinen om deras perspektiv för att komma vidare. Du kan också delta i interna utbildningsmöten där vi delar kunskap om allt från nya tekniker och arbetssätt till välmående. Vissa dagar avslutar du med AW, brädspel, filmkväll eller annan aktivitet du och kollegor tycker är kul.
 



  





**Din roll som Senior .NET Developer** 




 I rollen som Senior .NET Developer utformar och utvecklar du robusta och skalbara lösningar, från idé till produktion, för organisationer inom alla möjliga branscher från sjukvård till e-handel, transport och energi. En kollega beskriver sin roll som en “
 *Glue Engineer* 
 ”; du binder samman olika projekt genom att kombinera kundinteraktion, förundersökningar och kodning. Det roligaste med rollen enligt en annan kollega är “att få kombinera teknik och problemlösning: förstå hur olika delar hänger ihop, bygga något som verkligen används, och se helheten fungera i praktiken.”
 



  





 Du kan exempelvis vara med och modernisera och migrera en plattform, utveckla nya datadrivna produkter eller optimera systemutvecklingsprocessen med implementationer av AI- eller automationslösningar där de skapar som mest värde.
 



  





**Känner du igen dig i följande?** 




 Du kan kommunicera på svenska och engelska i tal och skrift. Det är ett plus om du varit konsult tidigare, det avgörande är din förmåga att snabbt komma in i nya sammanhang och lära dig. Tack vare flera års erfarenhet av att bygga moderna lösningar genom hela utvecklingscykeln, främst i C# .NET, känner du dig trygg i rollen då du fått med dig:
 


* Vana att jobba med distribuerade system och eventdriven, skalbar arkitektur. Gärna med tekniker som Kafka, RabbitMQ eller Azure Service Bus.
* God förståelse för Dev(Sec)Ops-principer, CI/CD och orkestrering.
* Erfarenhet av molnplattformar som Azure, AWS eller GCP. Har du jobbat med containerization (Kubernetes, Docker) eller Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Pulumi, Bicep) är det en extra tillgång.
* Nyfikenhet på AI i utvecklingsarbetet: hur verktyg som GitHub Copilot, OpenAI API:er eller ML-integrerade pipelines kan göra kodningen smartare och effektivare.



  





 Vi letar inte bara efter tekniska skills, vi vill jobba med dig som tar ansvar för att bygga rätt lösningar på rätt sätt genom att utmana med omtanke. Du ser kod som ett hantverk, inte bara ett medel till funktion: du bryr dig om kvalitet, läsbarhet och hållbarhet. Du kommer trivas hos oss om du tycker om att dela med dig av din kunskap lika mycket som du är nyfiken på- och lyfter andras.
 



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 Vår företagskultur är väldigt viktig för oss. Vi värdesätter låg hierarki, självständighet och ansvar, värdeskapande arbete och trevliga kollegor. Här kan du läsa mer om vår kultur, och här kan du bläddra bland våra tech-bloggar.
 


* **Meningsfulla projekt:** 
 Arbeta i en agil miljö där du kan påverka teknikval och arbetssätt för att skapa de bästa lösningarna
* **Flexibilitet och autonomi** 
 för att utforma ditt arbete med flextider– läs mer från vår blogg här.
* **Ständig utveckling:** 
 Vi erbjuder kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling för att säkerställa att våra medarbetare ligger längst fram i teknikutvecklingen.
* **Innovativ kultur:** 
 Arbeta tillsammans med några av de främsta experterna inom data, analys och utveckling – läs mer i bloggen här.



  





 Utöver detta får du även ta del av följande
 **förmåner** 
 :
 



 💰 Fast lön oberoende av beläggningsgrad med möjlighet till löneväxling till ditt pensionssparande
 



 🏦 Pensionsavsättning i linje med ITP1 samt möjlighet till löneväxling till ditt pensionssparande vid inkomst över 8,07 inkomstbasbelopp
 



 🏖️ 30 semesterdagar
 



 🏥 Sjukvårdsförsäkring för både din mentala och fysiska hälsa som täcker dig även utanför tjänstetid
 



 🏋️ Friskvårdsbidrag på 5000 kr via Benifex (tidigare Benify)
 



 🔗 Intern coach för att stödja ditt välbefinnande
 



 🪩 After Work och hobbyaktiviteter
 



 🥐 Fredagsfrukost
 



  





**Vilka är vi?** 




 Solita är ett värderingsstyrt konsultbolag med över 2200 medarbetare i 9 länder, varav ca 230 i Sverige. Vår storlek gör oss tillräckligt stora för att vara en stabil och innovativ arbetsgivare, men samtidigt tillräckligt små för att värna om våra medarbetare och kunder. Med bred kompetens inom digital transformation erbjuder vi en dynamisk arbetsmiljö. Hos oss får du friheten att utvecklas, både professionellt och personligt.
 



  





**Intresserad av att höra mer?** 




 Låter det som en intressant möjlighet? Skicka in din ansökan och bli en del av vårt team som ligger i framkant inom systemutveckling!
 



 Om du har några frågor, skriv gärna till Rebecca Hammel på LinkedIn eller via mail till rebecca.hammel@solita.se. Du kan också kontakta rekryteringsansvarig, Louise Doverholt på LinkedIn eller via mail till louise.doverholt@solita.se .
 



  





**Hur ser processen ut?** 




 I rekryteringsprocessen tror vi på en snabb och effektiv process. Du ansöker med CV eller LinkedIn och svarar på några korta frågor. Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande. Om vi ser en potentiell matchning, bjuder vi in dig till ett första möte för att lära känna dig och berätta mer om vår kultur och våra värderingar på Solita. I andra intervjun fokuserar vi på din tekniska kompetens och din erfarenhet. Om allt går bra tar vi oss vidare till sista steget där vi presenterar ett erbjudande och välkomnar dig som ny Solitan!

title: System Verification Engineer – Connectivity
description:
Are you an experienced System Verification Engineer who enjoys hands-on testing, fault tracing, and working close to real vehicles? Do you want to contribute to high-quality connectivity solutions within the automotive industry? Then this could be your next assignment.


ALTEN is now looking for a **System Verification Engineer** for an assignment with one of our clients within the automotive industry in Gothenburg. In this role, you will work with verification and testing of vehicle connectivity systems, ensuring robustness, quality, and readiness before release.

**About the role**


As a System Verification Engineer, you will work with end-to-end verification of vehicle connectivity solutions, both onboard and offboard. The role includes hands-on diagnostics and fault tracing in vehicles and system rigs, as well as development and execution of test cases based on system and vehicle requirements.


You will collaborate closely with internal teams as well as workshops and service organizations to coordinate and perform testing activities. The assignment combines structured verification work with practical, hands-on troubleshooting in complex automotive environments.



**Main responsibilities**

* Perform vehicle diagnostics and fault tracing in rigs and vehicles
* Execute end-to-end testing of onboard and offboard connectivity solutions
* Develop, execute, and report test cases on system and vehicle level
* Verify functionality according to requirements and quality targets
* Secure quality and readiness before release
* Contribute to continuous improvements of test methods and processes

**Your profile**


You are a structured and analytical engineer who enjoys hands-on verification work and system-level problem solving. You are curious, proactive, and comfortable working across different technical domains.


We see that you have:

* Minimum 5 years of experience in system verification within automotive environments
* Experience from verification in vehicles or system rigs (manual testing)
* Experience from working with connectivity
* B.Sc. or M.Sc. degree in Electrical Engineering, Software Engineering, or equivalent
* Experience with vehicle diagnostics and fault tracing
* Familiarity with requirement and system tools such as SystemWeaver
* Experience with Vector tools (e.g. CANalyzer, CANoe)
* Very good communication skills in English


Meritorious:

* A solid network within the automotive industry and understanding of development processes
* Experience working close to dealers or workshops
* T-shaped mindset with interest in learning new technical areas


We warmly welcome your application already today!

**About ALTEN**


ALTEN is one of Europe’s largest engineering and IT consulting companies with over 57,000 employees in around 30 countries. Our engineers carry out advanced projects throughout the product development cycle for leading companies in sectors such as Automotive, Aerospace, Energy, and Life Sciences.


In Sweden, we are about 1,300 committed employees across 9 cities – from Lund in the south to Skellefteå in the north. For the fifth consecutive year, ALTEN has been recognized as one of Sweden’s most attractive employers by Karriärföretagen, thanks to our strong focus on career development, innovation, and engineering excellence.


Visit **www.alten.se** to learn more about us.


  
JOBB DETALJER


 03/03/2026


Göteborg
REKRYTERING KONTAKT


 ALTEN Sweden

title: Electronics Engineer - Illumination & Power Outlet
description:
**We are looking for an** **Electronics Engineer - Illumination & Power Outlet for a global automotive company in Gothenburg. Start is ASAP, 1 year contract to begin with. This role is 100% onsite in Gothenburg.** **Assignment description**
Do you want to be part of creating the wow effect in our company? Then Illumination & Power Outlet team is the place for you. We have a really exciting future ahead of us where the Power Outlets develops (WPC, USB, 12V) in fast pace and with a changing design and technology. Illumination & Power Outlet team belongs to Interior System department where we develop the Displays, Wireless Phone Charger, USB, Interior Lights, Switches & Overhead Console. Illumination & Power Outlet teams are now looking for a HW responsible with electronics experience for our development project. The team consists of members with competence within lead engineering, test, software and hardware with a clear ambition to work cross-functional. We need talented people onboard. People with passion, energy, and drive to deliver good products for our customers. We are an organization that will support you to develop and reach your full potential.**What you will do**
You will work as HW responsible within our development teams.
As a HW responsible, you will lead the HW and electronic development by having close cooperation with our supplier, to secure that they deliver and follow our requirements to deliver a product with high quality. You will also be part of, for example, creating requirement specifications, evaluate HW performance in rig and actual vehicles, support in test activities as well as being part of packaging discussions to secure required space in future vehicles. In this role you will have close collaboration with the project leader, SW responsible and test engineer within your team. You will also have many other interfaces within VCC, for example, Interior, Design, EMC, Ergonomics, Electrical Performance, Perceived Quality, Vehicle Engineers and so on. This requires good ability to communicate with other people as well as being proactive, driven and dare to take your own initiatives.**You:*** Are structured and have a good sense of details.
* Are driven and result oriented.
* Are happy to share your best practices and equally happy to learn from others.
* Have great communication and collaboration skills
* Have interest in cars and driver experience

**And you have:*** A degree of M.Sc or B.Sc in electrical or computer science, or equivalent technical training/experience
* Min 3-4 years of Experience in electronic development.
* Experience in electronic requirement management.
* Experience from Automotive is meritorious
* Good English and Swedish skills, verbally and in writing
* Driving License: B (valid)


This is a full-time consultant position in Gothenburg through Incluso. Start is ASAP, 1 year limited contract to begin with, with possibility of extension after that. This role is 100% onsite in Gothenburg (Torslanda).

  

Please submit your CV as soon as possible since we will review the applications on an on-going basis.


For more information about this role, please contact Marianne Nilsson, recruiter at Incluso.

title: ML System Integration and Test Engineer
description:
**Job ID** 2025-16188


**Date posted** Mar. 03, 2026


**Location** Lund, Sweden


**Category** Software Engineering


  
We are looking for an experienced software/hardware engineer with a strong analytical approach to join the team and help ensure the best quality with most recent Arm ML software, systems and IP.


The successful engineer will be highly flexible, quick to learn and be motivated by the opportunity to understand and improve the quality of future Machine Learning solutions using Arm technology.


Are you our next team member?


Job Description:


As an ML Delivery engineer you will develop test infrastructure for our fully automated test flow, including functional and performance testing, as well as visualisation and report generation of the results.


We use advanced pre-silicon platforms of next-generation systems, to understand new use-cases and significant workloads to ensure Arm IP and systems deliver excellent ML performance and quality.


Required Skills and Experience :

* We’re looking for someone who follows up when something seems off, takes ownership beyond their assigned tasks, and pushes past team limitations to drive the right outcome.
* Great **python** knowledge is essential.
* Comfortable developing on Linux or Mac
* You have experience working with SW development or automated testing.
* You have strong communication skills, inter-cultural awareness and you embrace diversity.


“Nice To Have” Skills and Experience :

* Experience from building test infrastructure
* Experience working with pre-silicon platforms.
* Familiarity with **Docker** and **CI/CD systems** (Jenkins, GitLab, or GitHub).
* Understanding of **ML frameworks, compilers, or model execution flows**.
* Familiarity with data analysis.


In Return:


At Arm, you will enjoy working in a highly stimulating collaborative environment. Our team works closely with other software, hardware and system teams across the company.


You will have a chance to share ideas with and learn new skills from the best engineers in the world. We work in small teams, so your contributions will really make a difference.


#LI-CM1


Accommodations at Arm


At Arm, we want to build extraordinary teams. If you need an adjustment or an accommodation during the recruitment process, please email accommodations@arm.com. To note, by sending us the requested information, you consent to its use by Arm to arrange for appropriate accommodations. All accommodation or adjustment requests will be treated with confidentiality, and information concerning these requests will only be disclosed as necessary to provide the accommodation. Although this is not an exhaustive list, examples of support include breaks between interviews, having documents read aloud, or office accessibility. Please email us about anything we can do to accommodate you during the recruitment process.


Hybrid Working at Arm


Arm’s approach to hybrid working is designed to create a working environment that supports both high performance and personal wellbeing. We believe in bringing people together face to face to enable us to work at pace, whilst recognizing the value of flexibility. Within that framework, we empower groups/teams to determine their own hybrid working patterns, depending on the work and the team’s needs. Details of what this means for each role will be shared upon application. In some cases, the flexibility we can offer is limited by local legal, regulatory, tax, or other considerations, and where this is the case, we will collaborate with you to find the best solution. Please talk to us to find out more about what this could look like for you.


Equal Opportunities at Arm


Arm is an equal opportunity employer, committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal opportunities are available to all applicants and colleagues. We are a diverse organization of dedicated and innovative individuals, and don’t discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

title: Project Product Quality Engineer
description:
**For this recruitment we are collaborating with Academic work. Please submit your application via** **Senior Product Quality Engineer to Mycronic! | Academic Work** **.**

Mycronic is a global high-tech company that has been driving the electronics industry forward for over 50 years. We continue to grow and support customers across an increasing number of industries, and what we do shapes the technologies that define the society of the future - how we live our lives today and tomorrow. We are now looking for more people who want to join and strengthen our dedicated and driven teams at the forefront of technology. With us, you will work in an open, flexible, and collaborative environment where you collaborate closely with leading experts and take on challenging projects that turn ideas into reality and bring tomorrow’s electronics to life.


**About the role**  

Mycronic continues to grow across a wide range of industries, and the Pattern Generator (PG) division, which you will belong to, currently holds a unique position in the market as the world’s leading supplier of mask writers for the production of advanced photomasks. Every high-resolution display you see - whether it’s a TV, phone, tablet, or Apple Watch, has been manufactured with the help of Mycronic’s machines as part of the production process.


As a Senior Product Quality Engineer, you will be part of the Product Quality Center department, consisting of eight highly skilled team members with strong expertise in compliance and quality. In addition to your immediate team, you will work closely with our R&D organization, including mechanical, mechatronics, and electronics design engineers, our software teams, project managers, architects, and production. Our products within the PG division are highly technically complex and consist of many interacting subsystems, placing significant demands on quality and risk management. With an increased pace of development, higher production volumes, and shorter lead times, structured and proactive quality work is becoming increasingly important. In this role, you will have the opportunity to take on a key and central position in identifying and preventing risks early in our development projects. You will help ensure that risk analyses are carried out at the right time, with the right depth, and that quality insights and lessons learned from deviations and incidents are translated into long-term, preventive improvements in the way we work.


This is a role for someone who wants to make a real difference in a technically advanced environment, where quality, system understanding, and continuous improvement are crucial to our continued success.


**Work tasks**  

Mycronic currently has a strong operational delivery capability, and your mission will be to further strengthen the systematic quality work by driving high-quality root cause analyses, creating structure, and ensuring that our quality requirements are not only defined but also followed in practice. You will also contribute to improving Ways of Working for proactive product quality by identifying improvement areas in day-to-day operations and proposing concrete, value-adding enhancements in close collaboration with the Product Quality Area Manager and the Product Quality Center.


For example, you will:


* Ensure that Critical To Function / Critical To Quality criteria are identified, documented, analyzed, and managed systematically, and conduct DFMEA or Change-FMEA in new development and product enhancement projects.
* Capture and structure “tacit knowledge” from engineers, system architects, operations, and the service organization, and challenge assumptions when risks are known but not clearly addressed.
* Ensure clear formulation of risks, mitigation actions, and verification plans.
* Ensure that insights from deviations and issues are reflected in updated risk analyses and future design work.
* Secure a system and lifecycle perspective by integrating cross-functional input from R&D, Operations, and the service organization.
Facilitate effective root cause workshops and coach project, development, operations, and service teams in their execution.  
* 

**We are looking for**  

Mycronic offers a role for someone who thrives in a high-tech environment and in a culture where colleagues both challenge and support one another. We believe you have the ability to elevate our processes to the next level, guide the organization, and maintain a long-term perspective. In addition, you are someone who takes initiative and actively builds networks to create results and drive change. You have a strong sense of how to challenge others constructively, encouraging reflection and demonstrating the value of a more focused and structured quality approach, without slowing down our pace of development.


*In addition, you have:*

* A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in electronics, mechanical engineering, mechatronics, or a related field
* Several years of professional experience in quality, with documented experience applying xFMEA, DfX, and structured Root Cause Analysis (RCA) in real projects
* Proven experience applying Design for Manufacturing, Testability, and Serviceability (DfM, DfT, DfS) in development and redesign work
* Experience working with complex products that include both hardware and software
* Experience collaborating with R&D, production, and service organizations
Excellent English skills, both spoken and written  
* 

*It is considered an advantage if you:*

* Have experience from development projects involving low-volume products
Are fluent in Swedish  
* 

**Our recruitment process**

Phone interview with Academic Work
  

Personality- and problem-solving test
  

In depth interview with Academic Work
  

Interviews with Mycronic
  

Reference checking and decision  



**Other information**

Location: Headquarters in Täby, Stockholm
  

Extent: Fulltime, permanent employment
  

Contact information: Senior Recruitment Consultant Johanna Sörell, johanna.sorell@academicwork.se
  

This recruitment process is managed by Academic Work, and Mycronic’s request is that all questions regarding the position are directed to Academic Work.  

  



**Mycronic AB (publ)**  

Mycronic is an equal opportunity employer committed to workplace diversity and inclusion. We are committed to ensuring and cultivating a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. Operating in a global market like we do, it is a necessity to have a diverse workforce to serve our customers that are spread across different markets and cultures. Diversity, equity and inclusion is important for our innovation power and for staying competitive in a rapidly changing world and marketplace.


Read more about Mycronic on our website: https://www.mycronic.com/  



**For this recruitment we are collaborating with Academic work. Please submit your application via** **Senior Product Quality Engineer to Mycronic! | Academic Work** **.**

title: Ingenjör med industrierfarenhet
description:
### **JOB DESCRIPTION**



Sigma Industry Evolve erbjuder tekniska konsulttjänster till såväl stora som små industrikunder runt om i regionen. Vi söker nu en erfaren ingenjör till vårt sammansvetsade team i Uppsala. Sigma erbjuder trygga arbetsvillkor samt att vi lägger stor vikt vid den personliga utvecklingen.



Om du har en akademisk bakgrund och ett tekniskt intresse är du i gott sällskap här på Sigma. Du kommer arbeta från vårt kontor i Uppsala samt ute på plats hos kund i deras verksamhet. Resor till och från uppdrag förekommer som en naturlig del i arbetet. För att lyckas i rollen tror vi att du är praktiskt lagd och du räds inte att lära dig nya saker. Du har sedan tidigare ett antal års relevant arbetslivserfarenhet som exempelvis ingenjör eller tekniker.



**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter**



* Projektledning
* Planering & koordinering
* Metod- och processförbättringar
* Effektivisera cykeltider och flöden
* Utreda kvalitetsavvikelser
* Industrialisera nya produkter
* Implementera ny utrustning
* Minska kassation och realisera kostnadsbesparingar
* Ta fram tekniska kravspecifikationer
* Kommunicera med leverantörer och intressenter


**Kvalifikationer**



* Utbildad civil-/högskoleingenjör inom maskin, kemi, produktion, industri, energi eller motsvarande
* Tidigare erfarenhet från produktion, processindustri, underhåll, drift, projektledning eller tekniska roller
* Utmärkta kunskaper i svenska och engelska i såväl tal som skrift är ett krav


Vidare är det extra meriterande om du har tidigare erfarenhet från energibranschen eller verkstadsindustri. Du delar gärna med dig av kompetens och erfarenhet till andra. Det är också önskvärt med tidigare erfarenhet inom projektledning eller att leda andra i arbetet.



**Företagsbeskrivning**
  

Sigma Industry Evolve AB är ett teknikkonsultföretag inom process-, produkt- och produktionsutveckling som erbjuder kvalificerade konsulttjänster till våra kunder. Med lång branscherfarenhet, ett stort engagemang samt fokus på kvalitet och hög effektivitet skapar vi en miljö som ger våra anställda möjlighet att utvecklas.

title: Systemingenjör
description:
### **JOB DESCRIPTION**



Har du spetskompetens inom Systems Engineering, med fokus på systemarkitektur, kravhantering och/eller cybersecurity.



I rollen som **Systems Engineer** har du en viktig roll i att omvandla tekniska koncept till innovativa och hållbara lösningar. Du kommer att arbeta mot kunds avdelning som utvecklar och levererar skräddarsydda, kompletta IT-lösningar efter kundens behov och specifikationer.



I din roll arbetar du i projektgrupper genom hela projektets livscykel; från idé till färdig lösning och i nära samarbete med både interna team och externa kunder. I ditt arbete realiserar du systemarkitekturen för att säkerställa att alla systemkomponenter samverkar effektivt i komplexa strukturer och krävande miljöer – med särskilt fokus på IT-infrastruktur och hårdvara. Du arbetar brett med allt från kravanalys och teknisk specifikation till kunddialog och dokumentationsgranskning.



**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter**



* Utforma och utveckla systemarkitektur på olika nivåer för att säkerställa skalbara och robusta lösningar
* Rekommendera och implementera tekniska lösningar för både interna och externa kunder
* Skapa och dokumentera systemarkitektur, och säkerställ att systemet uppfyller både tekniska och affärsmässiga krav.
* Samla in, analysera och dokumentera krav samt hantera kravbaser.
* Utarbeta systembeskrivningar och annan viktig dokumentation för att säkerställa spårbarhet och möta kundkrav


**Välj din inriktning**



Vi söker nu flera personer med olika spetskompetenser inom Systems Engineering.



**Systemarkitektur**



Du fokuserar på att designa och vidareutveckla systemarkitekturen med särskilt fokus på robust IT-hårdvara. Du ansvarar för tekniska vägval, strukturer och dokumentation och ser till att lösningarna är skalbara, återanvändbara och hållbara över tid.



**Kravhantering**



Du fångar in, analyserar och dokumenterar krav från olika intressenter, både interna och externa. Du skapar struktur, säkerställer spårbarhet och följer krav genom hela systemlivscykeln. Du fungerar som en brygga mellan teknik, verksamhet och kund.



**Cybersäkerhet**



Du bidrar till att våra system möter högt ställda krav på IT- och informationssäkerhet. Du arbetar med säkerhetskrav, hotmodellering, säker design och implementering, samt stödjer utvecklingsteam i att bygga säkra system från grunden.


### **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**


* Erfarenhet som systemingenjör med kunskap inom kravhantering och systemlivscykelprocesser (t.ex. INCOSE, ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288)
* Utbildning inom datavetenskap, teknik, mekatronik, elektronik eller motsvarande praktisk erfarenhet (t.ex. IT-infrastruktur eller driftsättning), gärna inom hårdvara
* Erfarenhet av kundkontakt samt vana att arbeta i projekt och tvärfunktionella team
* Erfarenhet av system safety eller ILS är meriterande
* Erfarenhet av modellering eller script (t.ex. Python, PowerShell) ses som ett plus

### **PREFERRED ADDITIONAL SKILLS, PERSONAL TRAITS AND EXPERIENCE**


* Du är nyfiken, drivande och affärsorienterad med ett tekniskt helhetsperspektiv
* Du har kunskap inom nätverk och IT-infrastruktur (hårdvara)
* God kommunikativ förmåga i både svenska och engelska

### **WE OFFER**



*Sigma Industry Evolution AB är ett företag som arbetar inom med utveckling i flera branschen och områden. Vi är ett personalutvecklingsbolag, det är här vårt fulla fokus ligger, gå med i ett företag som är helt fokuserat på dig och det du behöver.*



*Kombinationen av lång branscherfarenhet, stort engagemang, interna och externa uppdrag samt nära samarbete mellan Sigmas olika verksamheter i regionen skapar ett unikt branschöverskridande växthus för ingenjörer. Med mångfald, delaktighet och entreprenörskap stärker och utvecklar vi vår verksamhet och våra medarbetare. Skulle du vilja utvecklas i den här miljön?*

title: Material and modelling engineer
description:
**JOB VACANCY:** MATERIALS & MODELLING ENGINEER
  
Company Overview
  
Impetus (IMPETUS Afea) is a leading expert company in computational mechanics, best known for developing the Impetus Solver — a high-precision, GPU-accelerated platform for simulating extreme mechanical events.
  
Our technology is used in applications ranging from impact and fragmentation to structural response and fluid-structure interaction. With offices in Flekkefjord (NO) and Stockholm (SE), we work at the intersection of advanced physics, engineering insight, and real-world defence applications.
  
We are now expanding our team with a new kind of role.
  
**Role:** Materials & Modelling Engineer
  
This is a hybrid position combining computational modelling, physical material testing, educational model building and visual and written communication.
  
You will help transform complex physics into intuitive models, teaching material, and documentation that make advanced simulation accessible and useful.
  
Your work will contribute both to internal knowledge building, external manuals/training material and experimental material characterization.
  
What You Will Do
  
  

**You will work in a split role between:**  

1. Educational & Demonstration Modelling
  
Build clear, instructive simulation models using Impetus Solver
  
  

**Develop example cases for:**  

Manuals
  
Training material
  
Application notes
  
Customer onboarding
  
Help visualize complex material behavior and physical phenomena
  
Contribute to documentation that explains why, not just how
  
  

2. Materials & Testing Work
  
Participate in material testing activities
  
Support calibration of material models
  
Connect experimental observations with simulation models
  
Help translate physical behaviour into usable modelling approaches
  
  

Who You Are
  
  

**We are looking for someone with:**  

Background in materials science, engineering physics, or similar
  
Experience using Impetus Solver
  
Excellent written and spoken English
  
Strong ability to communicate technical ideas clearly
  
  

Experience
  
Previous work experience is not required. This role may suit a recent graduate, a PhD candidate, or someone early in their career.
  
What matters most is understanding of materials behavior, familiarity with Impetus Solver and a strong communication ability.
  
Location
  
Stockholm, Sweden
  
Interested?
  
Send your CV, cover letter, and references to tom.inge@impetus.no with the subject line 'Software/Simulation Engineer Application – Impetus'. We look forward to exploring how your skills and ambition align with our mission to drive precision in simulation.

title: CARE FOR POTENTIAL TRAINEE PROGRAM
description:
**FÖRETAG**


Stena Aluminium AB

**PLATS**


Älmhult, Sverige

**SISTA ANSÖKNINGSDATUM**


2026-03-22

**YRKESOMRÅDE**


Studerande



  
**Care for Potential trainee program 26/27 – join us!**



Are you ready to take on real responsibility early in your career – and develop into a future key player within Stena Aluminum. Do you want to use your potential, drive results, and help develop smart solutions that contribute to a more sustainable society?



Apply to our trainee program Care for Potential.



The Care for Potential Trainee Program is designed to fast-track ambitious graduates into key operational leadership roles. We are looking for high-potential individuals who combine business acumen with the drive to create measurable results – and who want to build a long-term career within the Stena Metall Group, which Stena Aluminium is a part of.


**Apply for a trainee position at Stena Aluminium in Älmhult.**



As a Trainee at Stena Aluminium, you’ll have the opportunity to turn your academic knowledge into hands-on experience, participate in relevant training programs, expand your professional network, and build valuable understanding of our operations and how we create impact. You will work with experienced colleagues in operations and the goal is clear- after completing the 10-month program, you will step into a Project Engineer/Project Manager role, with continued development toward senior operational leadership. You will have great opportunities to influence and develop our processes, technical solutions, and ways of working within a rapidly evolving organization. You will become part of a journey where innovation, sustainability, and technology go hand in hand.



The program starts in September 2026. We have one position open in Älmhult.


**What makes this program different?**



At Stena Aluminium, operations are at the core of our business. This is where profitability, sustainability, and customer value come together.



During the program, you will:


* Combine structured training with real business responsibility. We will challenge you to unleash your full potential.
* Participate in modules focused on operational excellence, financial performance, leadership, and sustainability.
* Build an international network with trainees and colleagues across Sweden, Finland, Norway, Poland and Italy.
* Work directly with experienced managers who will mentor and challenge you.
* Contribute to a company that plays a key role in accelerating the transition to a circular economy.


We offer permanent employment from day one and a market-based salary. Click here to here learn more about the Program.


**Qualifications**


* You have completed a technical university degree, e.g. engineering (Bachelor or Master).
* You have a maximum of 2 years of relevant full-time work experience after completing your degree.
* Fluent in Swedish and in English, both written and spoken.
* Resident within commuting distance of Älmhult.


But more importantly:


* You are motivated by responsibility
* You enjoy being close to operations, where decisions have direct impact.
* You are curious and eager to learn.
* You bring a strong interest in technology, software development and system integration, production, sustainability and innovation.
* You are comfortable taking initiative even when the path forward is not fully defined.
* You combine analytical strength with the ability to turn ideas into practical action.
* You aspire to lead people and create engagement in teams.
* You build trust through strong interpersonal skills and understand that lasting results are created through collaboration and clear communication.


**Application and recruitment process**



We welcome your application no later than March 22. The selection process includes assessments, reference checks, and background- and drug screening. If you have any questions throughout the process, you are welcome to contact Sofie Tunekil at People & Culture, sofie.tunekil@stenaaluminium.com.



Ready to take the first step towards your new career? It starts with you.


**About Stena Aluminium**

title: Sales Engineer
description:
SOTI is committed to providing its employees with endless possibilities; learning new things, working with the latest technologies and making a difference in the world.
Job Title: Sales engineer
Location: Gothenburg, Sweden  

Who We Are
At SOTI, we are committed to delivering best in class mobile and IoT device management solutions. We are looking for out of the box thinkers that appreciate the art of creating great software.
To us, being visionary is more important than doing things the way they’ve always been done.  

What’s in it for you?*The People -* Our people are our most important resource. From our humble origins in our founder’s basement, to our industry leading position today, SOTI has worked hard to foster a company culture that we can all believe in. A culture that emphasizes personal growth, continuous innovation and fun.*The Growth -* Our environment fosters new ideas, fresh perspectives, and the ability to take them over the goal line. SOTI is a fast-paced environment with a global reach that encourages you to make your mark and be part of something big!*The Technology* - You’ll get the chance to work with leading edge technologies and take on complex and interesting projects, as part of highly collaborative and agile teams. You will work alongside SOTI’s partners which include leading tech giants that will keep you on the cusp of emerging technologies.  

SOTI’s growth and success is a result of commitment to innovation through entrepreneurial culture and leadership. Employees are driven to make an impact, offer a unique value and most importantly, be part of a winning team.  

You have a great passion for modern technology, and you are driven by your energy to identify and resolve complex problems, while providing excellent customer service. If you are eager to prove your understanding of computer networking principles, your strong problem-solving abilities in a fast paced environment, and you are willing to learn and grow, then this position offers numerous growth opportunities and a long-lasting career with SOTI. Pre-Sales Engineers are responsible for satisfying the needs of new and existing customers in an effort to maximize their experience with SOTI products. This includes, providing technical support, revenue generation in add-on sales and professional service training and following up on support cases as needed. This position demands that one maintains a high level of customer service through all interactions as a SOTI representative.  

Responsibilities* Develop and grow revenue through consultative selling with clients and prospects, presenting a warm, professional, well-spoken and caring persona as an effective representative of SOTI
* Conduct onsite and remote demonstrations of SOTI products
* Provide pre-sales technical support to prospective customers
* Analyze SOTI's market segment and generate recommendations for product roadmaps
* Investigate issues reported by customers, and communicate with internal product development teams to resolve product issues
* Provide email and telephone based pre and post sales, technical support for our software products
* 20% travel is expected in this role.

  

Qualifications* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and any other Nordic language is meritorious
* Solid understanding of computer networking principles (Firewalls, Network Protocols, Subnetting, etc.)
* Knowledge of mobile computing platforms (Android, iOS and Windows Mobile)
* Vertical market expertise within the carrier market
* Able to plan, prioritize and organize workload, consistently working within service standards and agreed objectives
* Prior experience working with mobile device management solutions and enterprise mobility, BYOD, SaaS is an asset
* Ability to drive and support proof of concept (PoC) initiatives to demonstrate product value and align solutions with customer needs.
* Knowledge of Active Directory, certificates, CRM solutions is an asset
* Experience with databases, Microsoft SQL server, MY SQL, Oracle is an asset
* Experience with containerization and virtualization is an asset

  

About SOTI
SOTI is the world's most trusted provider of mobile and IoT management solutions, with more than 17,000 enterprise customers and millions of devices managed worldwide. SOTI's innovative portfolio of solutions and services provide the tools organizations need to truly mobilize their operations and optimize their mobility investments. SOTI extends secure mobility management to provide a total, flexible solution for comprehensive management and security of all mobile devices and connected peripherals deployed in an organization.  

At SOTI, we celebrate the uniqueness of our global teams and are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are curious problem solvers who are committed to bringing the best mobile and IoT management solutions to market.  

We offer careers with #EndlessPossibilities.
#LI-HJ1
If you want to bring your ideas to life, apply at SOTI today.*Please note that SOTI does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of a signed Services Agreement with agency/recruiter, SOTI will not consider or agree to payment of any referral compensation or recruiter fee.*
company_description: SOTI is a proven innovator and industry leader for simplifying business mobility solutions.

title: Specialist System Engineer at Volvo Autonomous Solutions
description:
Transport is at the core of modern society. Imagine using your expertise to shape sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions for the future. If you seek to make a difference on a global scale, working with next-gen technologies and the sharpest collaborative teams, then we could be a perfect match.

 **Who are we?**


Volvo Autonomous Solutions (V.A.S) is the business area within Volvo Group that accelerates the development, commercialization, and sales of autonomous solutions. The vision for Volvo Group is to be the most desired and successful transport and infrastructure solution provider in the world. We don’t sell trucks and machines: we operate the solutions through the full life cycle to maximize the improvements achievable through autonomy.

  

That goes hand in hand with our long-term ambitions: 100% Safe, 100% Fossil free and 100% more productive. The automation of our vehicles is seen as a natural step towards reaching that goal. Joining the Volvo Group means contributing to a large global company that is transforming itself towards these ambitions. We are committed to diversity and inclusion, fostering a team culture where differences are seen as a stepping-stone to better results.

 **The Role**


In this role, you’ll join a highly skilled cross‑functional team where expertise in wiring harness development, hardware engineering, and system engineering comes together to create end‑to‑end autonomous functionality. This team is at the heart of enabling autonomous driving capabilities across the entire Volvo Group, and you will be a key contributor to that mission.

  

As an **Experienced System Design Engineer**, you will take the lead in developing and maintaining the autonomous vehicle interface—ensuring that software, hardware, and vehicle architecture come together in a seamless, robust system. You’ll help define how autonomous‑ready vehicles behave and interact in real‑world environments, translating that into clear system functionalities and documentation that guide development from concept to production.

  

Your work will span the full development chain: aligning requirements with internal and external partners, breaking down complex functional and non‑functional needs into actionable specifications, and contributing to the evolution from L2 to L3 autonomous capabilities. Drawing on your technical judgment, you’ll craft solutions that are efficient, scalable, and compliant with global regulatory standards.

  

A key part of the role involves reviewing system designs and ensuring that verification and validation plans meet the highest expectations. Staying connected to vehicle architecture, upcoming product roadmaps, and real customer use cases will help you maintain a holistic, forward‑looking perspective in everything you do.

  

Collaboration is central to your success here. You’ll work closely with software and hardware teams across the Volvo Group to deliver integrated autonomous features that are not only technically solid but also move the entire industry forward.

 **Your Expertise**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or related field.
* Minimum 6 years of experience in Electrical & Electronic (E/E) systems engineering.
* Strong understanding of systems engineering principles and development processes.
* Solid knowledge of electrical architecture within Volvo base platforms (or similar heavy vehicle platforms).
* Proven experience in cross-functional technical leadership.
* Theoretical understanding of Embedded C/C++; working knowledge of Python or scripting languages is an advantage.
* Experience with functional safety processes (e.g., safety analysis, concept design, requirement definition, and validation) is highly desirable.
* Familiarity with Agile/SAFe methodologies and tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
* Experience in ISO 26262 functional safety
* Experience in autonomous systems or ADAS
* Knowledge of cybersecurity (ISO 21434)
* Experience with requirement tools (e.g., DOORS, SystemWeaver)
* Experience in model-based systems engineering (MBSE)

 **What’s in it for you?**


We offer you the opportunity to work alongside highly skilled colleagues in a global and dynamic environment. We trust the individual, succeed as a team, and provide plenty of opportunities for continuous learning, growth, and career development.

 **Last application date:** 17th March

  

If you have any questions, Please reach out to Niclas Gustafsson, Head of Electrical Integration Design, niclas.gustafsson.2@volvo.com

  

We value your data privacy and therefore do not accept applications via mail.

 **Who we are and what we believe in**  

We are committed to shaping the future landscape of efficient, safe, and sustainable transport solutions. Fulfilling our mission creates countless career opportunities for talents across the group’s leading brands and entities.

  

Applying to this job offers you the opportunity to join **Volvo Group**. Every day, you will be working with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to be able to leave our society in better shape for the next generation. We are passionate about what we do, and we thrive on teamwork. We are almost 100,000 people united around the world by a culture of care, inclusiveness, and empowerment.

  

Part of Volvo Group, **Volvo Autonomous Solutions** accelerates the development, commercialization and sales of autonomous transport solutions, focusing on defined segments for the on- and off-road space. The combination of strong tech expertise and skilled customer solutions creates innovative transport offers never seen before. We are constantly pushing our own skills and ability to drive change in a traditional industry to meet a growing customer demand. We are now looking for innovative, committed individuals to join us in our endeavor to create customer solutions that enhance safety, flexibility and productivity.

  

 #LI-DNI
company_description: Every day, we work with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to leave our society in better shape for the next generation.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Craft:**
 Technology & Development
   

  

**Job Description:**
 Role: Full-stack Software Engineer
   

  

 Want to work with the world’s best mobile games? Have you got the skills to make the next big thing? We’re looking for a Full-stack Software Engineer to join our Kingdom. Our games are adored by hundreds of millions of people, and you’ll be helping to make them even better, as well as contribute to the engines, systems and platforms that make them play brilliantly on any platform.
   

  

**Your role within the Kingdom**
 If you want to create amazing user experiences and entertainment for millions of players to enjoy on the go then this is the right job for you. As part of the Central Web Tech team in Candy Crush Saga, together with your colleagues you will have a role in creating and maintaining brilliant gaming experiences for our players. You will design and implement existing and new game features.
   

  

**Your responsibilities will include:**
* Implementing and testing new or optimizing existing game features
* Taking an active part in feature creation
* Accountability for the technical metrics of the work items you deliver
* Contributing high-quality and well-structured code to our global code base


****Skills To Create Thrills****
 Programming experience and dedication for gaming as well as the willingness to share great ideas are equally valued.
   

  

* Full-stack development: Architecting scalable frontend and backend solutions using Typescript, React, Node.js, and Java
* Database management: Proficient in both SQL and NoSQL database design and optimisation
* API design: Expertise in building and optimising RESTful APIs, RCP and real-time communication via WebSockets
* Cloud infrastructure: Hands-on experience deploying and scaling applications on GCP
* Security & identity: Deep understanding of user authentication protocols, including JWT and OAuth
* Version Control: Expert use of GitHub for collaborative development and workflow management
* Responsive design: Optimising web applications for cross-platform performance, including WebViews and mobile-responsive layouts


**Additional bonus qualifications:**
* DevOps: Experience implementing CI/CD pipelines, specifically utilising GCP Cloud Build
* Mobile Development: Basic proficiency in native mobile development for Android (Java) and iOS (Swift)
* Experience in working with complex systems that interact with other features or parts of a project


**We also value**
* That you are passionate about games and would love to create the next big thing


***About King***
 With a mission of
